title: Ship session handoff notes and one-click resume context for uninterrupted drafting
Build persistent Story Library with create, rename, archive, and resume
description: ## Problem The app currently shows empty placeholders and does not let writers manage real stories. Without persistent story records and quick resume, users cannot build a daily writing habit or trust the app for ongoing projects. ## Target user Solo fiction writers drafting one or more long-form stories who need a reliable home screen and library to manage works-in-progress. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase weekly active writers who return to an existing story - At least 70% of writing sessions start from Quick Resume or Stories list after launch - Reduce first-session drop-off between story creation and first edit ## Scope - Add story persistence layer for metadata (title, updatedAt, archive flag) - Stories page supports create, rename, archive/unarchive, and delete-confirmation guardrail - Home dashboard shows Recent Stories and Quick Resume based on last edited items - Clicking any story opens `/story/[id]` for editing - Empty states include clear primary actions (Create story / Restore archived) ## Non-goals - Real-time multi-user collaboration - Publishing/sharing workflows - AI writing assistance ## Acceptance criteria - User can create a new story and see it appear in Stories and Home Recent list immediately - Renaming a story persists across reload and navigation - Archived stories are hidden from default list and recoverable from an archive filter - Quick Resume always points to most recently edited non-archived story - Deleting requires explicit confirmation and removes the story from all lists ## Priority rationale This is the minimum product surface required to make the app usable beyond a demo. It unlocks all downstream drafting and structure features by making stories first-class persisted objects. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Build a persistent Story Library that lets writers create, rename, archive, and resume stories with a resume-first Home dashboard, enabling daily writing habits and multi-project management. ## Scope and files **New files:** - `src/types/story.ts` - Story data model (title, id, updatedAt, isArchived) - `src/lib/stories.ts` - Story CRUD functions with localStorage persistence - `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.tsx` - Story card/row component for lists - `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.module.css` - Story card styles - `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx` - Reusable confirmation modal - `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.module.css` - Dialog styles - `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.tsx` - Inline action menu (rename, archive, delete) - `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.module.css` - Menu styles **Modified files:** - `src/app/page.tsx` - Wire up Quick Resume and Recent Stories with real data - `src/app/page.module.css` - Add styles for Resume button and story rows - `src/app/stories/page.tsx` - Add New Story button, story list, archive toggle - `src/app/stories/page.module.css` - Add toolbar button and list grid styles ## Step-by-step plan ### 1. Define Story data model and persistence layer Create `src/types/story.ts`: - Export `Story` interface with `id: string`, `title: string`, `updatedAt: number` (timestamp), `isArchived: boolean`, `createdAt: number` - Keep model minimal (no content field yet, only metadata) Create `src/lib/stories.ts`: - `getStories(): Story[]` - Load from localStorage `stories` key, return sorted by `updatedAt` desc - `getStory(id: string): Story | null` - Get single story by id - `createStory(title: string): Story` - Generate UUID, set timestamps, persist, return story - `updateStory(id: string, updates: Partial>): Story | null` - Merge updates, update `updatedAt`, persist - `deleteStory(id: string): void` - Remove from array, persist - All functions handle localStorage errors gracefully (try/catch with console.error) - Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for id generation - Storage key: `stories` ### 2. Build ConfirmDialog component Create `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx`: - Props: `isOpen: boolean`, `title: string`, `message: string`, `confirmLabel: string`, `onConfirm: () => void`, `onCancel: () => void`, `destructive?: boolean` - Render modal overlay with dialog (use semantic `` element or ARIA role) - Focus Cancel button by default (safe default) - ESC key triggers cancel - Backdrop click triggers cancel - Style with grayscale tokens, `--shadow-lg`, `--z-modal` Create `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.module.css`: - Overlay: fixed, full viewport, dark semi-transparent backdrop - Dialog: centered, white background, padding, border-radius-md, shadow-lg - Buttons: inline-flex, spacing-md gap - Destructive variant uses darker text for confirm button ### 3. Build StoryActionMenu component Create `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.tsx`: - Props: `storyId: string`, `storyTitle: string`, `isArchived: boolean`, `onUpdate: () => void` - State: `menuOpen: boolean`, `renameDialogOpen: boolean`, `deleteDialogOpen: boolean`, `newTitle: string` - Render overflow button (three dots or "···") that toggles menu - Menu options (conditional): - **Rename** (always): Opens inline prompt or dialog with text input pre-filled with current title - **Archive/Unarchive** (toggle based on `isArchived`): Calls `updateStory` immediately, closes menu, calls `onUpdate` - **Delete** (always): Opens ConfirmDialog with story title in message, on confirm calls `deleteStory`, calls `onUpdate` - Close menu on option select or outside click - Keyboard: menu items navigable with arrow keys, Enter to select, ESC to close Create `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.module.css`: - Menu button: icon button, hover state - Dropdown: absolute positioned, white bg, shadow-md, border - Menu items: block links/buttons, hover bg fog-200 ### 4. Build StoryCard component Create `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.tsx`: - Props: `story: Story`, `showActions?: boolean`, `onUpdate?: () => void`, `onClick?: () => void` - Render card with: - Story title (h4 or similar, clickable if `onClick` provided) - Last edited timestamp (formatted as "Last edited X hours/days ago" using relative time helper or hardcoded formatter) - Action menu (if `showActions` is true and `onUpdate` provided) - Entire card clickable if `onClick` provided (use keyboard-accessible button wrapper) - Focus state with 2px outline (--color-fashion-300) Create `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.module.css`: - Card: border, border-radius-md, padding-md, hover state (shadow-sm elevation) - Desktop: 2-column grid support via parent - Mobile: full-width stack - Typography: title uses --font-size-lg, timestamp uses --font-size-sm with --color-cement-400 ### 5. Update Home page for Quick Resume and Recent Stories Modify `src/app/page.tsx`: - Import `getStories` from `@/lib/stories` - Load stories in component (client component with `useState` + `useEffect` or Server Component if no interactivity needed; choose client for future optimistic updates) - Filter non-archived stories, sort by `updatedAt` desc - **Quick Resume card:** - If stories exist, show most recent (index 0): title, "Last edited X ago", and primary button "Resume writing" that navigates to `/story/[id]` - If no stories, show current empty state - **Recent Stories card:** - If stories exist, render list (max 5) of `StoryCard` with `onClick` navigating to `/story/[id]` and `showActions={true}` with `onUpdate` triggering re-fetch - If no stories, show current empty state - Add `router.push` navigation using `next/navigation` `useRouter` Modify `src/app/page.module.css`: - Add styles for `.resumeButton` (primary CTA: background --color-black-700, color white, padding, border-radius-md) - Add styles for `.storyList` (vertical stack with spacing-sm gap) - Add styles for `.timestamp` (--font-size-sm, --color-cement-400) ### 6. Update Stories page for create/list/archive Modify `src/app/stories/page.tsx`: - Convert to client component (`'use client'`) - Import `getStories`, `createStory` from `@/lib/stories` - State: `stories: Story[]`, `showArchived: boolean`, `isCreating: boolean` - `useEffect` to load stories on mount - Computed: `filteredStories = stories.filter(s => s.isArchived === showArchived)` - **Toolbar:** - Left: `New Story` button → calls `createStory("Untitled Story")`, navigates to `/story/[newId]`, sets `isCreating` loading state - Center: keep existing search input (not wired yet, placeholder for future) - Right: Archive toggle button (e.g., "Show Archived" / "Hide Archived") toggles `showArchived` - **Stories list:** - If `filteredStories.length > 0`, render grid/list of `StoryCard` with `onClick` and `showActions` - If empty and `!showArchived`, show "No stories yet" with "Create your first story to get started" - If empty and `showArchived`, show "No archived stories" with "Archive a story to see it here" - On story update (archive/rename/delete), refetch stories with `getStories()` Modify `src/app/stories/page.module.css`: - Add `.newStoryButton` (button styles, left-aligned in toolbar) - Add `.archiveToggle` (toggle button, right-aligned) - Add `.storiesGrid` (desktop: 2-column grid with gap, mobile: 1-column) - Add `.emptyState` refinements if needed (already exists, may need minor tweaks) ### 7. Wire story navigation In `StoryCard` and Home/Stories pages: - Use `next/navigation` `useRouter().push('/story/' + story.id)` for navigation - Ensure `/story/[id]/page.tsx` can handle any valid UUID id (it currently does) ### 8. Add accessibility and keyboard support - All interactive elements must be keyboard-navigable (buttons, menu items, dialogs) - Focus management: dialog opens → focus Cancel, dialog closes → return focus to trigger - ARIA: dialog has `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby` for title, `aria-describedby` for message - Screen reader: action menu announces "Actions for [story title]" - Test tab order: toolbar → story cards → action menus ### 9. Test and polish - Manual test: Create story, rename, archive, unarchive, delete (with confirmation), resume - Test edge cases: empty states, long titles (truncation?), very old timestamps - Test persistence: reload page, check localStorage in DevTools - Test responsive: desktop 2-col, tablet/mobile 1-col - Test keyboard: tab through all controls, activate with Enter/Space ## Test plan **Unit/integration tests (manual):** 1. **Create story:** - Click "New Story" on Stories page - Verify navigates to `/story/[id]` with new UUID - Go back, verify story appears in Stories list and Home Recent Stories 2. **Rename story:** - Click action menu on a story → Rename - Enter new title, confirm - Verify title updates in both Stories list and Home - Reload page, verify persistence 3. **Archive story:** - Click action menu → Archive - Verify story disappears from default list - Toggle "Show Archived", verify story appears - Unarchive, verify returns to main list 4. **Delete story:** - Click action menu → Delete - Verify confirmation dialog appears with story title - Cancel, verify nothing deleted - Delete again, confirm, verify story removed - Reload, verify deletion persisted 5. **Quick Resume:** - Create/edit multiple stories - Verify Home shows most recently edited story in Quick Resume - Click "Resume writing", verify navigates to correct story 6. **Empty states:** - Delete all stories - Verify Home shows "No stories yet" / "Nothing in progress" - Verify Stories shows "No stories yet" with create CTA - Toggle archive, verify "No archived stories" 7. **Persistence:** - Open DevTools → Application → Local Storage - Verify `stories` key exists with JSON array - Manually corrupt JSON, reload, verify graceful error handling (empty list) **Accessibility tests:** - Tab through entire Home and Stories pages, verify all buttons/menus reachable - Open action menu, navigate with arrow keys, select with Enter - Open delete dialog, press ESC, verify closes - Test with VoiceOver/NVDA: verify announcements for action menu, dialog **Responsive tests:** - Desktop (1024px+): 2-column story cards, single-row toolbar - Tablet (768-1023px): 1-column stories, toolbar may wrap - Mobile (<768px): 1-column, full-width buttons **Commands:** ```bash npm run dev # Manual testing in browser at http://localhost:3000 # Test all CRUD operations, navigation, persistence, accessibility ``` ## Risks and mitigations **Risk 1: Data loss due to localStorage limits or corruption** - Mitigation: Wrap all localStorage operations in try/catch, log errors to console - Future: Add localStorage quota check, warn user if nearing limit - Mitigation: Keep story metadata small (no content in library layer yet) **Risk 2: Race conditions with rapid create/update/delete** - Mitigation: Use synchronous localStorage reads/writes (blocking but simple for v1) - Future: Introduce optimistic UI with rollback on error - Mitigation: Disable action buttons during async operations (if we add API later) **Risk 3: Complex state management for archive filter + CRUD updates** - Mitigation: Keep state simple: single `stories` array, re-fetch on any mutation - Mitigation: Use `useEffect` dependencies carefully to avoid infinite loops - Future: Consider React Query or SWR for caching if performance issues arise **Risk 4: Accessibility gaps in custom dialog/menu components** - Mitigation: Follow WAI-ARIA dialog and menu patterns strictly - Mitigation: Test with keyboard only and screen reader before handoff - Mitigation: Use semantic HTML (``, `

title: Add manuscript import wizard for .docx/.txt with chapter-scene detection
description:
## Problem
Many target users already draft in Google Docs, Word, or plain text. Without import, switching costs are high and onboarding stalls before users experience the app's structure/planning advantages.

## Target user
Writers migrating an in-progress manuscript from general-purpose editors into a story-focused workspace.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase activation rate from signup to first meaningful edit session
- Reduce drop-off during first-time story creation
- Increase imported manuscripts that reach at least one saved revision inside app

## Scope
- Add an import entry point from Home/Stories (Import manuscript)
- Support .docx and .txt uploads up to a defined size limit
- Parse headings into chapter candidates with editable mapping before final import
- Create scenes from detected chapter sections with fallback to single-scene import
- Show import summary: chapters/scenes created, unresolved formatting warnings

## Non-goals
- Perfect formatting parity for every Word style
- PDF import
- One-click cloud provider sync integrations

## Acceptance criteria
- User can upload .docx or .txt and complete import without leaving app
- User can review/edit chapter mapping before finalizing import
- Imported manuscript opens in chapter/scene navigator with preserved text content
- Unsupported formatting is surfaced as non-blocking warnings, not silent data loss
- Failed imports provide clear, actionable error message and retry path

## Priority rationale
Import removes a major adoption barrier and accelerates progress toward the company goal by letting real writers bring real work into the app immediately.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

# Design Brief - Manuscript Import Wizard (BIN-68)

## Design Principle

**"Trust the Writer's Work"** - The writer has already done the hard part. The import wizard's job is to bring that work in accurately, let the writer fix anything the parser missed, and get out of the way. No data loss, no silent truncation, no surprises.

Market Research constraint: make structure conversion trustworthy and the "scary migration" feel safe and reversible. Confidence in the mapping step is the key differentiator over drag-and-drop file dumps.

---

## Import Entry Points

### 1. Home page
- "Import manuscript" button added next to "New story" on the Home page
- Position: secondary action button below the "New story" primary CTA
- Style: outlined button, `--color-carbon-600` text + border, no fill

### 2. Stories library page
- Same "Import manuscript" button in the toolbar alongside "New story"

### 3. Empty state inside story
- If a story has no content: add "or import from file" link below the empty textarea/editor

---

## Wizard Flow (3 steps)

The import wizard opens as a full-screen modal overlay (not a separate page):

```
Step 1: Upload
  ↓
Step 2: Review mapping
  ↓
Step 3: Import summary
```

Modal: `--color-white` bg, centered, max-width 720px, max-height 80vh, scrollable inner content, `--border-radius-lg` (12px), `--shadow-lg`

Progress indicator at top: 3 steps with connecting line, current step `--color-carbon-600`, completed `--color-elephant-500`, upcoming `--color-fog-200`

---

## Step 1: Upload

### Layout
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Import Manuscript                                      [×]  │
│  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  Step 1 of 3 — Upload your file                             │
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                                                      │   │
│  │         [upload icon]                                │   │
│  │   Drag and drop your manuscript here                 │   │
│  │   or click to browse                                 │   │
│  │                                                      │   │
│  │   Supported: .docx, .txt  ·  Max 10 MB              │   │
│  │                                                      │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                          [Cancel] [Next →]  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Drop zone:**
- Default: `--color-polar-100` bg, `2px dashed --color-fog-200` border, `--border-radius-md`
- Drag-over: `2px dashed --color-elephant-500` border, `--color-polar-100` bg
- File selected: show filename + size, swap icon for file icon, border becomes solid `--color-fashion-300`
- `--font-size-sm`, `--color-cement-400` for helper text

**File validation (inline, before Next):**
- Wrong type: "Only .docx and .txt files are supported"
- Too large: "File is too large. Maximum size is 10 MB."
- Empty file: "This file appears to be empty."
- All validation shown as inline error below drop zone, `--color-black-700` text on `--color-polar-100` bg

**"Next" is disabled** until a valid file is selected.

---

## Step 2: Review Mapping

This is the trust-building step. Show the parser's best guess and let the writer confirm or edit.

### Parsing logic (browser-side)

**.txt files:**
- Detect chapter breaks by: lines matching `/^(chapter|ch\.?)\s+\d+/i` or lines that are ALL CAPS and <= 30 chars, or lines preceded and followed by blank lines with 3+ chars
- Fallback: if no headings detected, create a single scene from full text content

**.docx files:**
- Parse heading styles (Heading 1, Heading 2) as chapter/scene breaks
- Fallback: same as txt if no heading styles found
- Use mammoth.js (browser-compatible DOCX parser) or a minimal custom parser
- Strip unsupported styles silently; collect them in a warnings list

### Mapping review layout

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Step 2 of 3 — Review chapter mapping                       │
│                                                              │
│  We detected 4 chapters in your manuscript.                  │
│  Rename or reorder before importing.                         │
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐       │
│  │  1  [Chapter 1: The Beginning       ×]  ~1,240 wds │     │
│  │  2  [Chapter 2: The Meeting         ×]  ~890 wds  │     │
│  │  3  [Chapter 3: The Turn            ×]  ~2,100 wds │     │
│  │  4  [Chapter 4: The End             ×]  ~750 wds  │     │
│  │  [+ Add chapter break]                             │     │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘       │
│                                                              │
│  ⚠ 3 unsupported formats will be imported as plain text     │
│                                                              │
│                              [← Back] [Import manuscript →] │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Chapter row:**
- Drag handle (same ⋮⋮ as corkboard) for reordering
- Editable title field: click to edit inline, `--font-size-sm`, `--color-carbon-600`
- Word count badge: `--color-cement-400`, `--font-size-xs` (computed from content length)
- Delete button (×): removes this chapter boundary; merges content into previous chapter

**"Add chapter break" button:**
- Opens a simple form: "Split before which line?" — shows a text excerpt and a slider/line picker
- This is an advanced affordance; not visible on mobile by default

**Fallback state (no chapters detected):**
- "We couldn't detect chapter breaks. Your manuscript will be imported as a single scene."
- Option: "Manually set chapter breaks" (advanced, collapsed by default)

**Warnings section:**
- If warnings exist: amber-adjacent note (using `--color-fashion-300` text, grayscale-safe)
- Text: "N unsupported formats will be imported as plain text"
- Expandable list: shows each warning (e.g., "Image on page 3 — not supported", "Track changes ignored")
- Non-blocking: writer can proceed regardless

---

## Step 3: Import Summary

### Success state

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Step 3 of 3 — Import complete                              │
│                                                              │
│  Your manuscript has been imported.                         │
│                                                              │
│  ✓  4 chapters created                                      │
│  ✓  4 scenes created                                        │
│  ✓  4,980 words imported                                    │
│  ⚠  3 formatting warnings (see details)                     │
│                                                              │
│  Story name:  [My Imported Novel          ]                  │
│  (You can rename this any time)                              │
│                                                              │
│                                  [Open in editor →]         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Story name: editable inline before opening; defaults to filename (without extension)
- Checklist items use `--color-elephant-500` for checkmarks, `--color-fashion-300` for warnings
- "Open in editor" button: primary style, navigates to story editor view (Editor mode)

### Error state

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Import failed                                              │
│                                                              │
│  We couldn't import your manuscript.                        │
│                                                              │
│  Error: File could not be parsed                            │
│                                                              │
│  What you can try:                                          │
│  · Save as .txt in your word processor and retry           │
│  · Check the file is not password-protected                 │
│  · Break the file into smaller sections (under 10MB)       │
│                                                              │
│                                  [Try again] [Cancel]       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Import into Existing Story

The import wizard should also support importing INTO an existing story:

- Available from the story page (e.g., a "More" menu or from the empty editor state)
- Step 1 and 2 identical
- Step 3 warns: "This will add N new scenes to your existing story. Existing scenes will not be affected."
- No merge/replace — append only in v1

---

## Component Inventory

### New components

| Component | File path | Purpose |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ImportWizard | src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.tsx | Modal wrapper, step routing |
| ImportUploadStep | src/components/ImportWizard/ImportUploadStep.tsx | File drop zone + validation |
| ImportMappingStep | src/components/ImportWizard/ImportMappingStep.tsx | Chapter review + edit |
| ImportMappingRow | src/components/ImportWizard/ImportMappingRow.tsx | Single chapter row (draggable) |
| ImportSummaryStep | src/components/ImportWizard/ImportSummaryStep.tsx | Success/error summary |

### New files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| src/lib/import.ts | parseDocx(file), parseTxt(file), detectChapterBreaks(text) — returns ParsedChapter[] |
| src/types/import.ts | ParsedChapter, ImportResult, ImportWarning types |

### Modified files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| src/app/page.tsx | Add "Import manuscript" button; show ImportWizard modal on click |
| src/app/stories/page.tsx | Add "Import manuscript" button in toolbar |
| src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx | Add "Import from file" affordance in empty editor state |

### CSS modules (new)

ImportWizard.module.css, ImportUploadStep.module.css, ImportMappingStep.module.css, ImportSummaryStep.module.css

---

## Dependencies

- **mammoth.js** (or equivalent) for .docx parsing: `npm install mammoth`
  - Browser-compatible, outputs HTML or raw text from DOCX
  - Version: latest stable (currently 1.x)
  - If mammoth is too large for bundle, use a minimal DOCX parser or fallback to .txt-only with a message

---

## Accessibility

- Modal: role="dialog", aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby="import-wizard-title", focus trap
- Drop zone: role="button", aria-label="Upload file: drag and drop or click to browse", keyboard activate (Enter/Space)
- File input: visually hidden but keyboard accessible (label for drop zone)
- Chapter rows: role="listitem", drag handle has aria-label="Drag to reorder chapter {n}"
- Edit title field: aria-label="Chapter {n} title"
- Warning list: role="list" with aria-label="Import warnings"
- Close (×): aria-label="Close import wizard"
- Step progress: aria-label="Step N of 3: {step name}", aria-current="step" on current

---

## Design Token Usage (all grayscale)

| Element | Token |
|---------|-------|
| Modal background | --color-white |
| Drop zone bg (idle) | --color-polar-100 |
| Drop zone border (idle) | --color-fog-200 (dashed) |
| Drop zone border (drag-over) | --color-elephant-500 (dashed) |
| Drop zone border (file selected) | --color-fashion-300 (solid) |
| Chapter row bg | --color-white |
| Chapter row hover | --color-polar-100 |
| Word count badge | --color-cement-400 |
| Warning indicator | --color-fashion-300 |
| Progress step completed | --color-elephant-500 |
| Progress step current | --color-carbon-600 |
| Progress step upcoming | --color-fog-200 |
| Import CTA button (primary) | --color-carbon-600 bg, --color-white text |

---

## Handoff

Assign to: Founding Engineer

</plan>

title: Ship a Revision Queue board that turns scene issues into ordered fix tasks
description:
## Problem
Writers accumulate revision notes across scenes, comments, and passes, but they lack a single execution queue that answers: what should I fix first, and why? This causes scattered edits, missed issues, and stalled revisions.

## Target user
Writers in revision phase managing many scene-level fixes across continuity, pacing, dialogue, and beta feedback.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of stories that move from draft complete to revision complete
- Reduce average time from first revision note to final pass completion
- Increase number of resolved scene-level revision tasks per active manuscript

## Scope
- Add a Revision Queue view that lists scene-level tasks with fields: issue type, severity, source, scene link, owner (self), and due order
- Allow task creation from existing inputs: manual notes, beta comments, and revision-pass outputs
- Provide queue sorting modes: severity, story order, and quick wins
- Add bulk actions: mark done, snooze, merge duplicate tasks
- Show progress summary: open tasks, overdue tasks, and completion trend for current manuscript

## Non-goals
- Real-time collaborative task assignment across teams
- General-purpose project management boards outside manuscript revision
- Auto-rewriting scene text

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create a revision task and link it to a scene
- Writer can convert at least one beta comment and one manual note into queue tasks
- Queue supports at least three deterministic sort modes (severity, story order, quick wins)
- Writer can mark tasks done and see progress metrics update immediately
- Clicking a queue item navigates to the correct scene context

## Priority rationale
We already have drafting and feedback surfaces; the missing step is execution discipline. A revision queue creates a concrete completion engine that helps writers finish stronger manuscripts, which improves retention and supports the company goal to ship an end-to-end writing workflow.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

# Design Brief - Revision Queue Board (BIN-101)

## Design Principle

**"Execution Discipline"** - The revision queue is not a notes list. It is a ranked, completable execution engine. Every interaction should move the writer closer to a done manuscript, not deeper into planning.

Market Research constraint: position as a manuscript completion system, not a generic task list. Sort modes and progress metrics are the primary differentiators.

---

## Where it Lives

Add a fourth view mode to the story page: **Editor | Corkboard | Revision | Submissions**

- ViewModeSwitch updated to include "Revision" tab
- Revision view lives at story/[id] in a new RevisionQueue component
- URL: `/story/{id}?view=revision` (add to existing view query param routing)

---

## Data Model

### RevisionTask type (new: src/types/revision.ts)

```typescript
interface RevisionTask {
  id: string;              // UUID
  storyId: string;
  sceneId: string | null;  // null = manuscript-level task, not scene-specific
  title: string;           // "Fix continuity gap in Act 2 opening"
  issueType: RevisionIssueType;
  severity: RevisionSeverity;
  source: RevisionSource;
  sourceRef: string | null; // original comment text or note excerpt
  status: 'open' | 'snoozed' | 'done';
  snoozedUntil: number | null; // unix timestamp
  createdAt: number;
  updatedAt: number;
}

type RevisionIssueType = 'continuity' | 'pacing' | 'dialogue' | 'characterization' | 'prose' | 'structure' | 'other';
type RevisionSeverity = 'critical' | 'major' | 'minor' | 'polish';
type RevisionSource = 'manual' | 'beta_comment' | 'self_note' | 'inline_comment';
```

**Persistence:** localStorage key `story-{storyId}-revision-tasks`

---

## Layout

### Two-column layout (desktop >= 1024px)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [Editor | Corkboard | Revision | Submissions]              │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─┤
│  REVISION QUEUE                    [+ Add task]           │ │
│                                                           │ │
│  Sort: [Severity] [Story order] [Quick wins]              │P│
│                                                           │R│
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │O│
│  │ [!] Fix continuity gap — Ch 2 Scene 3   [done]   │    │G│
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │R│
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │E│
│  │ [●] Slow pacing — Ch 1 Scene 1          [open]   │    │S│
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │S│
│                                                           │ │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─┘
```

- Main queue list: full width minus 48px progress sidebar
- Progress sidebar (right, 48px): vertical progress indicator (open/done ratio)
- Mobile: sidebar becomes a horizontal progress bar at top

---

## Queue Row Design

Each row shows one revision task:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [severity dot] Task title               [scene chip] [status]  │
│  Issue type · Source                                   [actions] │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Severity dot (left):**
- Critical: filled circle, `--color-black-700`
- Major: filled circle, `--color-elephant-500`
- Minor: open circle, `--color-fashion-300`
- Polish: open circle, `--color-fog-200`

**Task title:** `--font-size-sm`, `--color-carbon-600`, single line (truncate to 2 lines on expand)

**Scene chip:** `--font-size-xs`, `--color-cement-400`, `--color-polar-100` bg, shows scene title or "Manuscript" if no scene. Click to jump to scene (same nav as BIN-113).

**Status badge:**
- Open: no badge (implicit)
- Done: `--color-fog-200` bg, strikethrough on title
- Snoozed: `--color-fashion-300` text, "Snoozed" label

**Issue type + source:** `--font-size-xs`, `--color-cement-400`. Format: "Continuity · Manual note"

**Row actions (hover visible):**
- Mark done (checkmark icon)
- Snooze (clock icon) - opens snooze duration picker: 1 day / 3 days / 1 week
- Edit (pencil icon) - inline edit mode for title
- Delete (trash icon)

**Row hover:** `--color-polar-100` bg

**Done row:** reduced opacity (0.5), strikethrough title, collapsed to single line

---

## Sort Modes

Three mutually-exclusive sort buttons in the toolbar:

| Mode | Logic |
|------|-------|
| Severity | critical first, then major, minor, polish; ties broken by createdAt desc |
| Story order | sort by scene.order (manuscript order); no-scene tasks go last |
| Quick wins | minor + polish tasks first, then major, then critical; bias toward completable items |

- Default: Severity
- Persist selected sort to `story-{storyId}-revision-prefs` in localStorage
- Sort applies only to open tasks; done/snoozed tasks always go to bottom of their section

---

## Sections

Queue is divided into three collapsible sections:

1. **Open** (default expanded) — open tasks in current sort order
2. **Snoozed** (collapsed by default) — snoozed tasks, sorted by snoozedUntil asc
3. **Done** (collapsed by default) — completed tasks, sorted by updatedAt desc, limited to 20 shown

Section headers: `--font-size-xs`, `--font-weight-600`, `--color-cement-400`, all caps, with count badge

---

## Adding Tasks

### Manual task creation
- "+ Add task" button at top right of queue
- Expands an inline form row at the top of the Open section:
  - Title field (required, placeholder: "Describe the issue...")
  - Scene selector (searchable dropdown, shows all scenes, option for "Manuscript-level")
  - Issue type selector (chip buttons: Continuity, Pacing, Dialogue, Character, Prose, Structure, Other)
  - Severity selector (chip buttons: Critical, Major, Minor, Polish)
  - Source auto-set to "manual"
- Submit on Enter or "Add" button; cancel on Escape or "Cancel" button

### From inline comment
- Inline comments (BIN-125) get a "Add to revision queue" button in their tooltip
- Clicking it opens the add form pre-filled: sourceRef = comment text, source = "inline_comment", sceneId = current scene

### From beta feedback
- Beta comments (future: BIN-104) — interface defined but not wired yet
- Manual: any text field can be pre-filled by passing URL params `/story/{id}?view=revision&prefill=...`

---

## Progress Summary

### Right sidebar (48px, desktop)

Vertical bar chart showing open vs done ratio:
- Bar fill: `--color-elephant-500`
- Background: `--color-polar-100`
- Tooltip on hover: "12 open / 8 done / 3 snoozed"

### Header stats strip (above queue, below sort)

```
12 open  ·  8 done  ·  3 snoozed   →   40% complete
```

- `--font-size-xs`, `--color-cement-400`
- Completion % = done / (open + done + snoozed) * 100
- Updates immediately on mark-done

---

## Bulk Actions

Select mode:
- Checkbox appears on row hover (left of severity dot)
- Select any row to enter multi-select mode
- Bulk action bar appears at bottom of screen (sticky):
  `[N selected]  [Mark done]  [Snooze]  [Delete]  [Cancel]`
- Bulk action bar: `--color-carbon-600` bg, `--color-white` text, full width

---

## Empty State

**No tasks yet:**
- "No revision tasks yet"
- Subtext: "Add your first task, or import notes from your manuscript's inline comments."
- Button: "+ Add task"

**All done:**
- "All revision tasks complete"
- Subtext: "Your manuscript is ready for the next pass."
- Show count of completed tasks: "12 tasks resolved"

---

## Component Inventory

### New components

| Component | File path | Purpose |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| RevisionQueue | src/components/RevisionQueue/RevisionQueue.tsx | Main queue wrapper with sort + section management |
| RevisionTaskRow | src/components/RevisionQueue/RevisionTaskRow.tsx | Single task row with actions |
| RevisionAddForm | src/components/RevisionQueue/RevisionAddForm.tsx | Inline task creation form |
| RevisionProgressBar | src/components/RevisionQueue/RevisionProgressBar.tsx | Right sidebar + header stats |
| RevisionBulkBar | src/components/RevisionQueue/RevisionBulkBar.tsx | Sticky bottom bulk action bar |

### New files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| src/types/revision.ts | RevisionTask, RevisionIssueType, RevisionSeverity, RevisionSource types |
| src/lib/revision.ts | localStorage CRUD for revision tasks (getRevisionTasks, createRevisionTask, updateRevisionTask, deleteRevisionTask) |

### Modified files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx | Add revision view mode; route view=revision |
| src/components/ViewModeSwitch/* | Add "Revision" tab option |

---

## Accessibility

- Sort buttons: aria-pressed for active state
- Task rows: role="listitem", checkboxes have aria-label="Select task: {title}"
- Mark done: aria-label="Mark done: {title}"
- Snooze picker: role="menu" with keyboard navigation
- Section collapse toggles: aria-expanded
- Bulk action bar: role="toolbar", focus-trapped on open
- Progress bar: role="progressbar", aria-valuenow, aria-valuemax

---

## Design Token Usage (all grayscale)

| Element | Token |
|---------|-------|
| Queue background | --color-white |
| Row hover bg | --color-polar-100 |
| Row done bg | --color-white (opacity 0.5) |
| Severity critical dot | --color-black-700 |
| Severity major dot | --color-elephant-500 |
| Severity minor dot | --color-fashion-300 (open circle) |
| Severity polish dot | --color-fog-200 (open circle) |
| Scene chip bg | --color-polar-100 |
| Scene chip text | --color-cement-400 |
| Section header text | --color-cement-400 |
| Sort active button | --color-carbon-600 border + text |
| Progress bar fill | --color-elephant-500 |
| Progress bar bg | --color-polar-100 |
| Stats strip text | --color-cement-400 |
| Bulk bar bg | --color-carbon-600 |
| Bulk bar text | --color-white |

---

## Handoff

Assign to: Founding Engineer

</plan>

title: Link worldbuilding entities inline in the editor with mention chips and quick peek
description:
## Problem
Writers track characters, places, and lore in separate panels, but references inside prose are not structurally linked. This creates continuity drift and forces manual cross-checking during revision.

## Target user
Fiction writers managing recurring characters/locations/rules who need context while drafting without leaving the editor.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase usage of linked worldbuilding entities per manuscript
- Reduce continuity-related revisions tied to character/location inconsistency
- Increase frequency of worldbuilding panel interactions during drafting sessions

## Scope
- Add inline mention trigger in editor (e.g., @) to insert linked entity chips for character/location/rule items
- Store and render mentions as durable links to worldbuilding records
- Add hover/click quick peek card showing key entity details without full panel switch
- Provide mention management in scene metadata or inspector (list all mentions in current scene)
- Keep plain-text export fallback by converting mention chips to readable text labels

## Non-goals
- Automatic NER/entity detection from raw prose
- Complex bi-directional graph analytics in v1
- Multi-user mention resolution workflows

## Acceptance criteria
- User can insert entity mentions from the editor via keyboard and mouse
- Mention chips render consistently after reload and scene switches
- Clicking/hovering a mention opens a lightweight entity peek with core fields
- Removing an entity from worldbuilding does not corrupt manuscript text
- Export output remains readable when mentions are present

## Priority rationale
Campfire demonstrates demand for strong world context, but often in separate modules. Inline linking keeps the writer in flow and can become a differentiation point: context-rich drafting without UI sprawl.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Scene board with drag-and-drop cards
description:
Inspired by Plottr/Scrivener corkboard workflows.

Problem: writers lose narrative structure when scenes live in long documents.
Outcome: writers can visualize and reorder scenes quickly while preserving draft continuity.
Acceptance criteria:
- Scene board supports create/edit/delete scene cards with title, POV, status, and estimated word count.
- Drag-and-drop reordering updates manuscript order in the underlying draft model.
- Filtering by act/chapter/status is available and persists per user.
- Scene card to manuscript navigation opens at correct section.

Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

# Design Brief - Scene Board with Drag-and-Drop Cards (BIN-113)

## Design Principle

**"Single Source of Truth"** - The scene board and manuscript are two views of the same data. Every reorder, rename, or status change on the board must reflect immediately in the manuscript, and vice versa. There is no sync step, no save button, no version divergence.

Market Research constraint: emphasize fast bidirectional sync cues (board to manuscript), minimal interaction cost, and clear reorder confidence states.

---

## Baseline: What Already Exists

The corkboard component (src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx) already implements:
- Grid layout with @dnd-kit/sortable using rectSortingStrategy
- Drag-drop reordering with keyboard support
- Status filtering (all / planned / drafting / done)
- Scene cards with title, chapter, summary (3-line clamp), intent/POV chips, status dropdown, drag handle
- Empty state with 3 starter beats
- localStorage persistence (story-{storyId}-scenes key)

This brief covers the **gaps** needed to meet BIN-113 acceptance criteria:
1. Estimated word count display on cards
2. Scene card -> manuscript navigation (jump to correct section)
3. Act/chapter filtering with persistence
4. Bidirectional sync cues (board reflects manuscript changes)

---

## Scene Card Additions

### Estimated Word Count

Add word count to the card footer row alongside status.

**Display:**
- Label: `~450 wds` (approximate, not exact)
- Shown in card footer, right of status badge
- Token: `--color-cement-400`, `--font-size-xs`
- If word count is 0 or scene has no content: show `--` (not 0, which implies empty on purpose)

**Source:**
- Word count derived from scene's rich-text content in localStorage (story_{storyId}_scene_{sceneId}_content)
- Computed on card render, not stored separately. Use: `content.trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length`
- For legacy single-content stories (pre-BIN-125 migration), word count cannot be computed per-scene; show `--`
- Prefix `~` signals estimation, not a word processor count

**Edit behavior:**
- Word count is not manually editable. It updates automatically as the writer types in the manuscript.
- No click target on word count — it is read-only display.

---

## Scene Card -> Manuscript Navigation

### "Jump to scene" affordance

Each scene card gets a navigation trigger that opens the Editor view at that scene.

**Trigger:**
- On hover of scene card: a small "Open in editor" button appears in the top-right corner of the card
- Icon: external link or arrow-right glyph, 16px
- Background: `--color-white`, border: `1px solid --color-fog-200`, border-radius: `--border-radius-sm`
- Hover state on button: `--color-polar-100` bg
- Visible only on card hover (opacity 0 -> 1 transition, 150ms)
- Also keyboard accessible: Tab into card header area reaches this button

**Action:**
- Click: navigates to Editor view (`/story/{id}?view=editor&scene={sceneId}`)
- Editor reads `scene` query param on mount; scrolls to and expands that scene (see BIN-125)
- If Editor view is already active: smooth-scroll to scene + expand without page navigation

**URL param handling:**
- Add optional `scene` query param to story page routing
- On editor view init: if `?scene=...` present, scroll-spy to that scene ID

---

## Filtering Improvements

### Current state
Status filter: all / planned / drafting / done (toggle buttons in toolbar)

### New: Chapter/Act filter

Add a secondary filter row below the status filter:

```
STATUS:  [All]  [Planned]  [Drafting]  [Done]
CHAPTER: [All]  [Ch 1]  [Ch 2]  [Ch 3]  ...
```

- Chapter filter buttons are dynamically generated from the set of unique `scene.chapter` values
- If all scenes are in one chapter or no chapter set: chapter filter row is hidden
- Active filter: same visual treatment as existing status filter chips (border highlight)
- "All" is always first; chapter buttons sorted alphabetically

**Filter persistence:**
- Both status filter and chapter filter persist in localStorage key: `story-{storyId}-board-filters`
- Format: `{ statusFilter: 'drafting', chapterFilter: 'Chapter 1' }` (null means "All")
- Loaded on Corkboard mount; defaults to null/null (show all)

**Filter interaction:**
- Filters are AND-combined: must match both active status AND active chapter
- Click active filter button to deactivate (toggle off = show all for that dimension)
- When filter results in empty board: show empty state "No scenes match this filter" with "Clear filters" button

**Design tokens for filter bar:**
- Row gap: `--spacing-sm`
- Label ("STATUS:", "CHAPTER:"): `--font-size-xs`, `--color-cement-400`, all caps, right-padded `--spacing-sm`
- Chip (inactive): `--color-fog-200` border, `--color-elephant-500` text, `--font-size-xs`, `--border-radius-sm`
- Chip (active): `--color-carbon-600` border, `--color-carbon-600` text, `--font-weight-600`
- Chip hover: `--color-polar-100` bg

---

## Bidirectional Sync Cues

### Problem
When the writer renames a scene or changes its status in the manuscript editor, the corkboard card should reflect that change without requiring a page reload.

### Mechanism
- Both Corkboard and ManuscriptEditor read from the same localStorage key: `story-{storyId}-scenes`
- Use a `storage` window event listener in Corkboard to re-read scenes when another tab writes to this key
- Within the same tab: lift scene state to the story page level (`page.tsx`) and pass as props to both Corkboard and ManuscriptEditor

### Visual sync confirmation
- When a card updates from a manuscript edit: flash the card border `2px solid --color-elephant-500` for 800ms (CSS keyframe animation on a `data-synced` attribute)
- This signals "this card just changed" without being disruptive
- Flash only triggers on changes from the manuscript view (not from direct card edits)

### Reorder confidence
- During drag: dragged card shows `--color-polar-100` bg + `--color-fog-200` border (ghost)
- Drop zone: `2px dashed --color-fashion-300` border appears where card will land
- On drop: brief (300ms) `--color-polar-100` flash on reordered cards to confirm the new order
- Drag cancel (Escape): cards snap back with no flash

---

## Empty State (updated)

Current empty state shows 3 starter beats. Update to account for filtered empty state:

**No scenes at all:**
- Headline: "Your story has no scenes yet"
- Subtext: "Scenes are the building blocks of your manuscript."
- Buttons: "Add your first scene" + 3 starter beat quick-adds (unchanged)

**Filter produced no results:**
- Headline: "No scenes match this filter"
- Subtext: Shows current active filter (e.g., "Showing: Drafting in Chapter 2")
- Button: "Clear filters" (resets both filters to null)
- No starter beat buttons (scenes exist, just filtered out)

---

## Component Changes

### Modified: Corkboard

| Change | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Scene card word count | Add word count display to card footer |
| Scene card jump button | Add hover-visible "Open in editor" button |
| Chapter filter row | Dynamic chapter chips below status filter |
| Filter persistence | Read/write to localStorage on mount and change |
| Sync flash | CSS keyframe on data-synced attribute |
| Drop zone indicator | Dashed border on drag-over target |
| Empty state (filtered) | New "no match" empty state variant |

### Modified: SceneCard

| Change | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Footer row | Add word count `~N wds` display |
| Header hover | Add "Open in editor" button (opacity 0->1 on hover) |

### Modified: src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx

| Change | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Shared scene state | Lift scenes state up; pass to both Corkboard and ManuscriptEditor |
| scene query param | Read ?scene=... on mount; pass to ManuscriptEditor as initialFocusSceneId |

### Modified: src/lib/scenes.ts

| Change | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Filter persistence helpers | getStoredFilters(storyId), setStoredFilters(storyId, filters) |

### Modified: src/types/scene.ts

No changes needed to the Scene type. Word count is computed, not stored.

---

## Accessibility

- "Open in editor" button: aria-label="Open scene {title} in editor"
- Chapter filter chips: aria-pressed for active state (same pattern as existing status filter)
- Drag-and-drop: existing keyboard support unchanged (keyboard sensor already in place)
- Filter persistence: no accessibility impact (just localStorage read/write)
- Sync flash: uses CSS animation, not JS-triggered style changes (safe for prefers-reduced-motion: add @media query to skip flash)

---

## Design Token Usage (all grayscale)

| Element | Token |
|---------|-------|
| Word count text | --color-cement-400 |
| Jump button bg | --color-white |
| Jump button border | --color-fog-200 |
| Jump button hover bg | --color-polar-100 |
| Filter label text | --color-cement-400 |
| Inactive filter chip border | --color-fog-200 |
| Inactive filter chip text | --color-elephant-500 |
| Active filter chip border+text | --color-carbon-600 |
| Active filter chip weight | --font-weight-600 |
| Drag ghost bg | --color-polar-100 |
| Drop zone border | --color-fashion-300 (dashed) |
| Sync flash border | --color-elephant-500 |
| Post-drop flash bg | --color-polar-100 |

---

## Handoff

Assign to: Founding Engineer

</plan>

title: Add anchored beta-reader comments on shared chapter previews
Add universal command palette for instant manuscript navigation and actions
Launch encrypted cloud backup + cross-device sync with conflict-safe restore
Ship privacy-first AI consent controls with per-story opt-in
Version timeline with draft snapshots and restore
description:
Inspired by Google Docs version history and Sudowrite draft experiments.

Problem: writers fear large edits because rollback is difficult.
Outcome: writers can experiment safely and recover prior versions in seconds.
Acceptance criteria:
- Users can create named snapshots and browse automatic timeline checkpoints.
- Diff preview highlights inserted/deleted paragraphs before restore.
- Restore action creates a new head version without destructive overwrite.
- Snapshot metadata includes timestamp, author, and change summary.

Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

# Design Brief: Version Timeline with Draft Snapshots and Restore

## UX Vision

This is a **creative-safety feature**, not version control. Writers should think of it as "draft experiments" — a safe space to try bold edits knowing they can step back. Language, metaphors, and visual design should feel like a notebook with bookmarked pages, not a git log.

**Differentiation mandate (from Market Research):** Story-aware recovery. Snapshots and diffs are framed around the writing experience — prose readability, scene/chapter context — not file system semantics.

---

## Storage Schema

Extend existing `autosave.ts` localStorage pattern.

**New key:** `story_{storyId}_versions` → `VersionEntry[]`

```typescript
interface VersionEntry {
  id: string;           // crypto.randomUUID()
  content: string;      // Full text content at that point
  timestamp: number;    // Date.now()
  label: string | null; // null = auto-checkpoint, string = user-named draft
  type: "auto" | "named";
  wordCount: number;    // Derived at save time for quick display
}
```

**Retention policy:** Max 50 auto checkpoints + all named drafts. When over limit, drop oldest `auto` entries first. Named drafts are never auto-purged.

**Auto-checkpoint triggers:**
- Every 5 minutes during active editing (timer resets on each keystroke)
- Immediately before any restore action (safety snapshot)

**Named snapshot trigger:** User-initiated via "Save draft" button in history panel.

---

## Layout & Information Architecture

### Access Point
A **"History" button** in the story editor header (clock icon + label), positioned to the right of the Save Status indicator. Visible only when at least one auto-checkpoint exists.

### History Panel
A **right-side drawer**, 320px wide on desktop, slides in over the editor (editor dims to `opacity: 0.5` behind a `backdrop` overlay). Panel stays open while user browses; editor is non-interactive while panel is open.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Story Title]         [History] [Saved] │  ← Header
├──────────────────────┬──────────────────┤
│                      │  Draft History   │
│  [Editor content     │  ─────────────── │
│   dimmed / inert]    │  ◉ Current       │
│                      │  Named: "Before  │
│                      │    big rewrite"  │
│                      │  2 hours ago     │
│                      │  ─────────────── │
│                      │  Auto-checkpoint │
│                      │  4 hours ago     │
│                      │  1,240 words     │
│                      │                  │
│                      │  [+ Save draft]  │
└──────────────────────┴──────────────────┘
```

### Diff View
A **full-screen overlay modal** triggered by clicking "Preview" on a version entry. Shows the prose diff between that version and the current editor content. Single "Restore" CTA at the bottom.

---

## Component Specifications

### 1. `VersionHistoryButton`

**Location:** `src/components/VersionHistoryButton/`

**Appearance:**
- Icon: clock/history SVG (inline, 16×16)
- Label: "History"
- Style: ghost button, same visual style as existing header actions
- Disabled state: hidden when no versions exist yet

**Behavior:**
- Click → opens `VersionHistoryPanel`
- Keyboard: `Enter` / `Space` activates

---

### 2. `VersionHistoryPanel`

**Location:** `src/components/VersionHistoryPanel/`

**Structure:**
```
┌─ Draft History ──────────── [×] ┐
│                                 │
│  [+ Save named draft]           │  ← CTA at top
│                                 │
│  ── Today ────────────────────  │  ← Date group header
│  [VersionEntry]                 │
│  [VersionEntry]                 │
│                                 │
│  ── Yesterday ────────────────  │
│  [VersionEntry]                 │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Layout rules:**
- Width: 320px (desktop), full-width bottom sheet (mobile, <768px)
- Background: `var(--color-white)`
- Border-left: `1px solid var(--color-fog-200)`
- Shadow: `var(--shadow-lg)` on left edge
- Scrollable version list, header/CTA fixed

**Date grouping:** Group entries by calendar day using the `timestamp`. Show relative group headers: "Today", "Yesterday", then absolute dates (e.g. "March 12").

**"+ Save named draft" button:**
- Positioned at the top of the panel below the header
- On click: opens inline input field to name the snapshot, then saves
- Default placeholder: `"Draft · {formatRelativeTime(now)}"`

**Accessibility:**
- `role="dialog"`, `aria-label="Draft History"`
- Focus trap while open
- `Escape` closes panel, returns focus to History button
- Close button has `aria-label="Close draft history"`

---

### 3. `VersionEntry`

**Location:** `src/components/VersionHistoryPanel/VersionEntry.tsx` (co-located)

**Collapsed state (default):**
```
┌────────────────────────────────┐
│ ◉  [Named: "Before rewrite"]  │  ← Named badge or auto label
│    2 hours ago · 1,240 words   │  ← Metadata
└────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Expanded state (click to expand):**
```
┌────────────────────────────────┐
│ ◉  Before major rewrite        │
│    2 hours ago · 1,240 words   │
│                                │
│    [Preview diff]  [Restore]   │
└────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Visual states:**
- **Current version:** Pill label `Current` in `--color-carbon-600`, dot indicator filled `--color-black-700`
- **Named draft:** Label in `--color-elephant-500`, left border `2px solid --color-carbon-600`
- **Auto-checkpoint:** Label "Auto-saved" in `--color-cement-400`, dot outline only

**Word count:** Show as `{n} words` — familiar writer metric that signals how much content existed at that point.

**Hover:** Background `--color-polar-100`, cursor `pointer`

---

### 4. `VersionDiffView`

**Location:** `src/components/VersionDiffView/`

**Triggered by:** "Preview diff" button in an expanded `VersionEntry`.

**Layout:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ← Back to history        "Before major rewrite"      │
│                           2 hours ago · 1,240 words   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                      │
│  [Prose diff — unified view, paragraph-level]        │
│                                                      │
│  Legend: [▓ Removed]  [░ Added]                      │
│                                                      │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [Cancel]                    [Restore this draft →]  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Diff rendering (prose-friendly, grayscale):**

Granularity: paragraph-level (split on `\n\n`). This matches how writers think — in paragraphs, not lines.

| Change type | Visual treatment |
|-------------|------------------|
| Unchanged paragraph | Normal body text, `--color-black-700` |
| Removed paragraph | `--color-cement-400` text, `text-decoration: line-through`, `background: --color-fog-200` |
| Added paragraph | `--color-carbon-600` text, `font-weight: 500`, `background: --color-polar-100`, left border `3px solid --color-carbon-600` |

**"Restore this draft" button:**
- Full-width CTA at the bottom right, primary button style (`--color-black-700` background, `--color-white` text)
- On click → confirmation inline (no modal-on-modal): button becomes "Confirm restore" (red tint not available; use bold label change + secondary text "A new draft will be created with this content")
- On confirm → close diff view → close history panel → editor updates to restored content → save state shows "Restored from [label]"

**"Back to history" link:** Top-left, text link style, returns to panel without closing overlay.

**Scroll:** Diff content is scrollable; header/footer are fixed.

**Accessibility:**
- `role="dialog"`, `aria-label="Preview draft diff"`
- Focus trap
- `Escape` → back to panel (not close everything)
- Removed/added paragraphs have `aria-label="Removed paragraph"` / `aria-label="Added paragraph"`

---

### 5. `SnapshotNameInput` (inline in panel)

Not a separate modal — inline in the panel header area.

**Behavior:**
1. Click "+ Save named draft"
2. Input appears below the button with placeholder `"Name this draft…"`
3. `Enter` → saves and dismisses input, new entry appears at top of list
4. `Escape` → cancels without saving
5. Empty submit → auto-generates label `"Draft · {relative time}"`

---

## Hook: `useVersionHistory`

**Location:** `src/hooks/useVersionHistory.ts`

```typescript
interface UseVersionHistoryResult {
  versions: VersionEntry[];
  saveNamedVersion: (label: string) => void;
  restoreVersion: (versionId: string) => void; // creates new head, updates editor
  previewVersion: (versionId: string) => VersionEntry | null;
  isHistoryOpen: boolean;
  openHistory: () => void;
  closeHistory: () => void;
}
```

This hook:
- Reads/writes `story_{storyId}_versions` via a new `versions.ts` lib
- Handles auto-checkpoint timer (5-min interval, clears on unmount)
- `restoreVersion()` saves a safety snapshot before restoring, then updates editor content via callback

---

## Integration into `story/[id]/page.tsx`

Minimal changes to the existing page:
1. Add `useVersionHistory(id, content, setContent)` hook
2. Add `<VersionHistoryButton>` in the header next to `<SaveStatus>`
3. Render `<VersionHistoryPanel>` and `<VersionDiffView>` conditionally (portalled to document body)
4. When a restore happens: set content in editor, trigger immediate save

---

## New Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/lib/versions.ts` | CRUD for version entries in localStorage |
| `src/hooks/useVersionHistory.ts` | Hook managing open state, auto-checkpoint timer, restore |
| `src/components/VersionHistoryButton/VersionHistoryButton.tsx` | Header trigger button |
| `src/components/VersionHistoryButton/VersionHistoryButton.module.css` | Styles |
| `src/components/VersionHistoryPanel/VersionHistoryPanel.tsx` | Drawer panel + VersionEntry sub-component |
| `src/components/VersionHistoryPanel/VersionHistoryPanel.module.css` | Styles |
| `src/components/VersionDiffView/VersionDiffView.tsx` | Full-screen diff preview modal |
| `src/components/VersionDiffView/VersionDiffView.module.css` | Styles |

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add hook + render new components
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — dim overlay styles for editor backdrop

---

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping

| Criterion | Design element |
|-----------|---------------|
| Create named snapshots | "+ Save named draft" with inline `SnapshotNameInput` |
| Browse automatic timeline checkpoints | `VersionHistoryPanel` with date-grouped `VersionEntry` list, auto-checkpoint entries |
| Diff preview highlights inserted/deleted paragraphs | `VersionDiffView` with paragraph-level, prose-friendly grayscale diff |
| Restore creates new head version (non-destructive) | "Restore this draft" → confirm → new head created, editor content updated |
| Snapshot metadata: timestamp, author, change summary | `VersionEntry` shows relative time + word count; label field covers summary |

---

## Risks & Constraints

- **localStorage size:** Full content stored per version. Mitigate with 50-entry auto cap and no inline binary data.
- **Diff readability for long prose:** Paragraph-level granularity avoids overwhelming line-level diffs. Consider max visible paragraphs with "show all" toggle.
- **Mobile layout:** Panel becomes a bottom sheet; diff view goes full-screen. Ensure touch scroll works inside scrollable containers.
- **No word-level diff in v1:** Paragraph granularity is simpler and prose-friendlier. Word-level can be added in v2.

## Handoff

Assign to: Founding Engineer

</plan>

title: Character bible linked to manuscript mentions
description:
Inspired by Campfire and World Anvil reference systems.

Problem: character details drift over long drafts, causing continuity errors.
Outcome: writers maintain consistent character traits and relationships while drafting.
Acceptance criteria:
- Character profiles support core fields (bio, goals, relationships, appearance, voice notes).
- Manuscript mentions auto-link to matching character entries with manual override.
- Continuity warnings flag conflicting details (e.g., eye color mismatch) before export.
- Character search and quick panel are available during editing.

Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

# Design Brief — Character Bible Linked to Manuscript Mentions (BIN-115)

## Overview

This feature turns the character list from a passive reference wiki into an **active continuity guardrail**. The core thesis from Market Research: auto-linking mentions plus conflict warnings surfaced *inside* the drafting flow reduces revision debt in a way most competitors leave manual.

Design priorities (per Market Research recommendation):
1. **Low-noise warnings** — only surface conflicts with meaningful confidence; don't interrupt flow
2. **Manual override clarity** — any auto-link must be trivially dismissible or reassignable
3. **Confidence indicators** — communicate certainty so writers trust the system

---

## 1. Information Architecture

### New Route
- `/story/[id]/characters` — Character Bible page (character list + detail panel)

### Navigation Integration
- Add `characters` as a third option in `ViewModeSwitch` alongside `editor` and `corkboard`
- ViewMode type expands to: `'editor' | 'corkboard' | 'characters'`
- In editor view, add a **Characters panel toggle** button in the header toolbar (right side, near SaveStatus) — reveals a slide-out panel without leaving the editor

---

## 2. Component Inventory

### New Components

| Component | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `CharacterBible` | Full-page view — list + detail layout |
| `CharacterCard` | Summary card in the list view (name, avatar initials, role tag) |
| `CharacterDetail` | Right-panel detail: all profile fields |
| `CharacterMentionChip` | Inline editor decoration for auto-linked mentions |
| `CharacterQuickPanel` | Slide-out panel accessible from editor — shows character search + cards |
| `CharacterSearch` | Search input + filtered results list, reused in bible and quick panel |
| `ContinuityWarningBanner` | Pre-export warning roll-up (severity-grouped) |
| `ContinuityWarningTooltip` | Hover/focus tooltip on a flagged mention in the editor |

### Modified Components

| Component | Change |
|---|---|
| `ViewModeSwitch` | Add `characters` tab |
| Story `page.tsx` | Render `CharacterBible` in characters mode; add quick panel toggle + overlay |
| Story `page.module.css` | Panel overlay and slide-out animation styles |

---

## 3. Character Profile Fields

All fields optional. Stored in localStorage under `characters:{storyId}`.

```
id, storyId, name (required), aliases[], role (protagonist|antagonist|supporting|minor),
bio, goals, appearance, voiceNotes, relationships[{ characterId, label }], createdAt, updatedAt
```

**Aliases** are critical — auto-linking must check all aliases, not just `name`, to catch nicknames ("Ellie" -> Eleanor).

---

## 4. UX Flows

### 4a. Character Bible (Full View)

**Layout:** Two-column on desktop (>=1024px). Left: scrollable character list + add button. Right: character detail panel.
- Mobile/tablet (< 1024px): single column, tapping a card navigates to detail (back button returns to list)
- Empty state: centered placeholder with "Add your first character" CTA

**List panel:**
- Characters sorted by role priority: protagonist first, then antagonist, supporting, minor, then alphabetical within role
- Each `CharacterCard` shows: initials avatar (--color-fog-200 bg, --color-carbon-600 text), name, role badge, first line of bio (truncated 1 line)
- "+ New Character" button at top (full-width, dashed border style — same as existing "new" patterns)

**Detail panel:**
- Inline editable fields — click to edit, auto-save on blur (reuse `useAutosave` debounce pattern)
- Field sections: Identity (name, aliases, role), Appearance, Personality (bio, goals, voice notes), Relationships
- Relationships: tag-style chips showing linked character name + relationship label (e.g., "sister of Maya"). Add via inline combobox.
- Destructive actions (delete character) behind `ConfirmDialog` — reuse existing component

### 4b. Auto-Linking Mentions in Editor

**Detection:** On content change (debounced 800ms after typing stops), scan editor text for exact matches against all character names and aliases (case-insensitive). Match must be a whole word (word-boundary regex).

**Visual treatment:** Matched mentions get a `CharacterMentionChip` — subtle underline using `border-bottom: 1.5px solid var(--color-elephant-500)` (not a heavy highlight — low visual noise). Do NOT change text color.

**Interaction on chip click/hover:**
- Small popover appears (--z-popover) showing character name, role badge, one-line bio excerpt
- Popover actions: "View full profile" (opens quick panel focused on this character) and "Unlink" (dismisses auto-link for this instance, stored in content metadata)
- Popover is keyboard accessible (Tab to reach, Enter/Space to trigger, Escape to close)

**Confidence:**
- High confidence: name is unique across all characters — no indicator
- Low confidence: name matches multiple characters or is also a common word — chip shows a "?" badge (--color-cement-400) with tooltip "Matched to [Name] — click to change"

**Manual override:** Writer can:
1. Unlink: removes chip decoration, stored as annotation so it stays unlinked on future scans
2. Relink to different character: shows a mini dropdown of all characters

### 4c. Character Quick Panel (in Editor)

- Toggled by a panel icon button in the header toolbar (right of ViewModeSwitch)
- Slides in from the right, 320px wide, overlays the editor (does not reflow editor content)
- Contains: search input at top, scrollable list of `CharacterCard` (compact variant)
- Clicking a card expands an in-panel detail summary (name, role, bio, appearance — read-only)
- "Open in Bible" link navigates to full character bible view
- Panel closes on Escape or clicking the backdrop overlay

### 4d. Continuity Warnings

**Scope for v1:** Detect conflicts between character appearance/trait fields and free text in the manuscript. Specifically:
- Eye color: if `appearance` field contains "blue eyes" but manuscript text contains "[name]'s brown eyes"
- Name consistency: if character is renamed in the bible, flag old-name occurrences in manuscript

**When shown:**
1. `ContinuityWarningBanner` appears above the editor content area (below the toolbar) when warnings exist — **dismissible per session**
2. On the conflicting mention in the editor, a `ContinuityWarningTooltip` shows on hover — small amber-tinted border (`border-bottom: 1.5px dashed var(--color-cement-400)`) distinguishable from the normal mention underline
3. Pre-export: if export feature exists, show a modal summary of all warnings

**Warning severity:**
- **Informational** (gray): possible match, low confidence — "Possible name match"
- **Warning** (medium gray, dashed): trait conflict detected, medium confidence
- Authors can "Dismiss" individual warnings (stored in localStorage, not re-triggered)

**No alert colors:** Stay within the grayscale token set. Use dashed vs solid underlines and "?" vs "!" badges to communicate severity without color-coded urgency. This avoids overwhelming the writing environment.

---

## 5. Visual Design Specs

### Character Card
```
- bg: --color-white, border: 1px solid --color-fog-200
- border-radius: --border-radius-md
- padding: --spacing-md
- avatar: 36x36px circle, bg --color-fog-200, text --color-carbon-600, --font-size-sm --font-weight-semibold
- name: --font-size-base --font-weight-medium --color-black-700
- role badge: inline-block, --font-size-xs, --color-cement-400, border 1px solid --color-fog-200, --border-radius-sm, padding 2px --spacing-xs
- hover: bg --color-polar-100, border-color --color-fashion-300
- selected/active: bg --color-polar-100, border-left: 3px solid --color-carbon-600
```

### Mention Chip (in editor)
```
- No background change
- border-bottom: 1.5px solid var(--color-elephant-500)
- cursor: pointer
- On hover: border-bottom slightly darker
- Conflict variant: border-bottom: 1.5px dashed var(--color-cement-400)
```

### Character Quick Panel
```
- width: 320px
- position: fixed, right: 0, top: [header height], bottom: 0
- bg: --color-white
- border-left: 1px solid --color-fog-200
- box-shadow: --shadow-lg
- z-index: --z-popover
- slide-in animation: transform translateX(100%) to translateX(0), 200ms ease-out
```

### Continuity Warning Banner
```
- bg: --color-polar-100
- border-bottom: 1px solid --color-fog-200
- padding: --spacing-sm --spacing-md
- text: --font-size-sm --color-elephant-500
- dismiss button: text style, --color-cement-400
```

---

## 6. Accessibility

- All interactive chips are `<button>` or wrapped with `role="button"` + appropriate `aria-label` (e.g., `aria-label="Character mention: [Name] — click to view or unlink"`)
- Quick panel: `role="dialog"` with `aria-label="Character quick reference"`, focus trapped while open, Escape closes
- `ConfirmDialog` reuse ensures delete confirmations meet WCAG dialog requirements
- Character form fields: labeled with `<label>` elements, not placeholder-only
- Continuity warning banner: `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` so screen readers announce when warnings appear
- Role badges: `aria-label` includes full role text

---

## 7. Data Model (localStorage)

```typescript
interface Character {
  id: string;           // crypto.randomUUID()
  storyId: string;
  name: string;
  aliases: string[];
  role: 'protagonist' | 'antagonist' | 'supporting' | 'minor';
  bio: string;
  goals: string;
  appearance: string;
  voiceNotes: string;
  relationships: { characterId: string; label: string }[];
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}

interface MentionOverride {
  offset: number;       // character offset in content
  characterId: string | null;  // null = unlinked
  isManual: boolean;
}

interface ContinuityWarningDismissal {
  storyId: string;
  warningKey: string;   // deterministic hash of warning type + context
  dismissedAt: string;
}
```

Storage keys:
- `characters:{storyId}` -> `Character[]`
- `mention-overrides:{storyId}` -> `MentionOverride[]`
- `warning-dismissals:{storyId}` -> `ContinuityWarningDismissal[]`

---

## 8. New Files

```
src/types/character.ts
src/lib/characters.ts                  (CRUD helpers, alias-aware lookup)
src/lib/mentionScanner.ts              (text scanning, match detection, confidence scoring)
src/lib/continuityChecker.ts           (trait conflict detection)
src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterBible.tsx
src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterBible.module.css
src/components/CharacterCard/CharacterCard.tsx
src/components/CharacterCard/CharacterCard.module.css
src/components/CharacterDetail/CharacterDetail.tsx
src/components/CharacterDetail/CharacterDetail.module.css
src/components/CharacterMentionChip/CharacterMentionChip.tsx
src/components/CharacterMentionChip/CharacterMentionChip.module.css
src/components/CharacterQuickPanel/CharacterQuickPanel.tsx
src/components/CharacterQuickPanel/CharacterQuickPanel.module.css
src/components/CharacterSearch/CharacterSearch.tsx
src/components/CharacterSearch/CharacterSearch.module.css
src/components/ContinuityWarningBanner/ContinuityWarningBanner.tsx
src/components/ContinuityWarningBanner/ContinuityWarningBanner.module.css
```

## 9. Modified Files

```
src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx   (add 'characters' tab)
src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx                        (wire CharacterBible, QuickPanel toggle)
src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css                 (panel overlay, slide animation)
```

---

## 10. Handoff Notes for Founding Engineer

- The mention scanner runs **client-side only** using regex — no NLP library needed for v1. Word-boundary matching is sufficient.
- Conflict detection in `continuityChecker.ts` should be pattern-based (field value substring vs manuscript text substring), not semantic. False positives are acceptable if they are dismissible.
- The `CharacterQuickPanel` overlay should NOT reflow the editor text — use `position: fixed` so the editor width is unchanged and writers don't lose their place.
- Auto-save for character profiles reuses the 2s debounce pattern from `useAutosave`. Do not create a new autosave hook — pass `storyId` and a character-specific storage key.
- `mentionScanner.ts` must be debounced (suggested: 800ms after last keypress) to avoid scanning on every keystroke.
- All token-only styling — no new colors outside `tokens.css`.

</plan>

title: Add scene context metadata (POV, location, timeframe) with filterable navigator
description:
## Problem
Writers lose narrative orientation when drafting across many scenes because key context (POV, location, timeframe) is not consistently visible or searchable. This increases continuity mistakes and slows revision.

## Target user
Multi-chapter fiction writers who juggle multiple POV characters, locations, and timelines while drafting and revising.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce continuity-related scene revisions per manuscript
- Increase weekly active use of scene navigator filters
- Decrease average time to find target scene during revision

## Scope
- Add scene-level metadata fields: `POV character`, `location`, `timeframe` (free text v1 with optional suggestions from existing worldbuilding entities)
- Surface metadata in scene cards/navigator rows and at top of scene editor
- Add filter controls in chapter/scene navigator for POV, location, and timeframe
- Keep metadata editable from both scene settings and scene card quick-edit
- Persist metadata for use in existing timeline/continuity workflows

## Non-goals
- Automatic contradiction detection rules
- Complex ontology management for tags/entities
- AI-generated scene metadata

## Acceptance criteria
- User can set/edit POV, location, and timeframe on any scene
- Scene navigator can filter by each metadata field independently and in combination
- Active filters are clearly visible and can be reset in one action
- Metadata appears in the scene editor header without obstructing writing flow
- Metadata values persist across reload and are available in timeline views

## Priority rationale
This builds a practical writing-flow advantage aligned with Living Writer/Campfire inspiration: keep structure context visible while drafting, not hidden in separate planning screens. It also compounds value of in-flight navigator and timeline features already on the roadmap.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

## Goal
Add scene-level context metadata (POV character, location, timeframe) with visible in-editor display and filterable corkboard navigation.

## Scope and files

**Modified files (6):**
1. `src/types/scene.ts` - Add optional metadata fields: `pov?`, `location?`, `timeframe?`
2. `src/lib/scenes.ts` - Add `updateSceneMetadata` helper function
3. `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` - Add metadata row to editor, wire up metadata updates
4. `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` - Metadata row styles
5. `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx` - Add filter state and filtering logic
6. `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx` - Add metadata display line

**New files (4):**
1. `src/components/MetadataRow/MetadataRow.tsx` - Metadata input fields for editor
2. `src/components/MetadataRow/MetadataRow.module.css` - Metadata row styles
3. `src/components/MetadataFilters/MetadataFilters.tsx` - Filter controls for corkboard
4. `src/components/MetadataFilters/MetadataFilters.module.css` - Filter styles

## Step-by-step plan

### Phase 1: Data model extension

**Step 1.1: Add metadata fields to Scene type**
- File: `src/types/scene.ts`
