<plan>

# Design Brief - Chapter-Aware Manuscript Editor (BIN-125)

## Design Principle

**"The Long-Session Flow"** - This is not another rich-text editor. It is the best environment for uninterrupted, long-form fiction drafting. Every design decision filters through one question: does this help a writer stay in the story for hours without friction?

Market Research constraint: distraction-minimized, keyboard-first navigation, clear scene boundaries, predictable collapse/expand. Ship as the best long-session drafting flow, not a feature-complete document editor.

---

## Layout Architecture

### Three-panel layout (desktop >= 1024px)

- **Left sidebar (Scene Navigator):** replaces the current placeholder "Notes" sidebar. 240px fixed width on desktop, hidden/drawer on mobile. Chapter-grouped scene list for jump navigation.
- **Manuscript column:** centered, max-width 720px, comfortable reading/writing measure. Continuous scroll of all scene sections.
- **No right sidebar** in this version - keep the writing surface uncluttered.

### Mobile (< 768px)
- Scene Navigator accessible via a "Scenes" button that opens a full-height drawer overlay.
- Manuscript column is full-width.
- Floating action bar at bottom: fold/unfold current, add scene break, open navigator.

---

## Scene Section Design

Each scene renders as a discrete section in the manuscript column, ordered by scene.order.

### Scene Header (always visible, even when folded)

- Chapter badge: `--color-cement-400` text, small caps, `--font-size-xs`
- Scene title: editable inline (click to edit), `--font-size-lg`, `--font-weight-600`, `--color-carbon-600`
- Status badge: same chip style as Corkboard (planned / drafting / done)
- Fold toggle: right-aligned, `--color-fashion-300`, toggles section body visibility
- Bottom: `1px solid --color-fog-200` separator line

### Scene Body

**Expanded state:**
- Prose content area with rich-text editor
- Background: `--color-white`
- Font: Georgia / Times New Roman (existing serif stack), `--font-size-md` (16px), `--line-height-relaxed` (1.75)
- Max-width: 720px, centered
- Padding: `--spacing-xl` top/bottom, `--spacing-lg` sides

**Collapsed state:**
- Show `scene.summary` - 2 lines max, italic, `--color-cement-400`
- Background: `--color-polar-100` tint
- Collapsed height ~48px
- Click collapsed body to expand AND restore cursor to last position

### Active scene visual treatment
- Cursor inside scene: left border `3px solid --color-elephant-500`
- No other scenes get this treatment simultaneously

---

## Rich-Text Controls

**Philosophy:** Markdown-like shortcuts only. No visible toolbar by default. Toolbar appears on text selection (floating) or via `Mod+/`.

### Supported formatting

| Format | Keyboard shortcut | Use in fiction |
|--------|------------------|----------------|
| Bold | Cmd/Ctrl+B | Character names, emphasis on first use |
| Italic | Cmd/Ctrl+I | Primary emphasis, internal thoughts, foreign words |
| Scene break (* * *) | Cmd/Ctrl+Enter | Section break within a scene |
| Clear formatting | Cmd/Ctrl+backslash | Remove all marks from selection |

**Deliberately excluded:** underline, strikethrough, headings, lists, tables, block quotes, colors.

### Inline Comment (revision note)
- Highlight text + Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+M to attach a note
- Background: `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` tint on highlighted text (grayscale only)
- Comment shown in tooltip on hover; superscript dagger marker in `--color-cement-400`
- Stored in localStorage; not exported with manuscript

### Floating selection toolbar
Appears 8px above selection when 1+ chars selected: **[ Bold ] [ Italic ] [ Comment ]**
- Background `--color-carbon-600`, text `--color-white`, `--border-radius-sm`
- Disappears on click-outside or Escape; z-index `--z-popover` (1400)

### Markdown input shortcuts
- `**text**` / `__text__` converts to bold
- `*text*` / `_text_` converts to italic
- `---` on blank line converts to scene break divider

---

## Scene Navigator (Left Sidebar)

Chapter-grouped list of scenes with status dots. Click to scroll-jump in manuscript.

- Chapter header: `--font-size-xs`, `--font-weight-600`, `--color-cement-400`, all caps
- Scene item: `--font-size-sm`, `--color-elephant-500`, truncated, left-padded `--spacing-md`
- Active (scroll-spy): `--color-carbon-600` + `--color-polar-100` row bg
- Status dot (6px circle): planned=`--color-fog-200`, drafting=`--color-fashion-300`, done=`--color-elephant-500`
- Click: smooth-scroll to scene + expand if folded
- "Add scene" button: dashed border, `--color-cement-400`, full-width

### Keyboard nav
- Up/Down: move between scenes; Enter: jump; Escape: return to manuscript

---

## Scene Jump Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Shortcut |
|--------|---------|
| Next scene | Alt+Down |
| Previous scene | Alt+Up |
| Fold/unfold current | Alt+F |
| Focus Mode (fold all except current) | Alt+Shift+F |
| Expand all | Alt+Shift+E |
| Open navigator | Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+L |

Dismissable shortcut hint strip below scene header on first use. Not permanent chrome.

---

## Scene Folding Behavior

### Three states
1. **Expanded (default):** full prose visible and editable
2. **Collapsed:** header + summary only; body hidden via `display:none` (not `height:0` - avoids layout cost on long manuscripts)
3. **Focus Mode:** Alt+Shift+F collapses all scenes except the one with cursor

### Transitions
- Fold/unfold: `transition:none` - instant. Long manuscripts cannot afford reflow animations.
- Cursor restores to `lastCursorOffset` stored in component state (not persisted). New scenes start at position 0.

### Folded scene spec
- Background: `--color-polar-100`; left border: `2px solid --color-fog-200`; cursor: pointer
- Shows: chapter badge + title + status badge + expand arrow + summary (2 lines, italic, `--color-cement-400`)

---

## Content Persistence (Data Model Extension)

Replace single `story_${id}_content` with per-scene storage:

```
story_{storyId}_scene_{sceneId}_content
story_{storyId}_scene_{sceneId}_snapshot   (crash recovery)
story_{storyId}_scene_{sceneId}_metadata   (lastSaved, saveState)
```

- `useAutosave` hook extended to accept optional `sceneId`; derives storage keys per-scene.
- **Migration:** on first load, if `story_${id}_content` exists and no scene content exists, place it into the first scene's slot.
- Each scene autosaves independently on existing 2s debounce.
- SaveStatus shows aggregate: "All saved", "Saving...", or "N scenes unsaved".
- Recovery banner shows if any scene snapshot is newer than its metadata.lastSaved.
- Display order governed by `scene.order` (unchanged).

---

## Component Inventory

### New components

| Component | File path | Purpose |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ManuscriptEditor | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/ManuscriptEditor.tsx | Outer wrapper; renders ordered SceneSections, owns fold state map |
| SceneSection | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/SceneSection.tsx | Single scene: header + collapsible body |
| SceneHeader | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/SceneHeader.tsx | Chapter badge, editable title, status badge, fold toggle |
| SceneBody | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/SceneBody.tsx | Rich-text area (expanded) or summary (collapsed) |
| SceneNavigator | src/components/SceneNavigator/SceneNavigator.tsx | Left sidebar: chapter groups + scene list + scroll-spy |
| RichTextToolbar | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/RichTextToolbar.tsx | Floating selection toolbar |
| SceneBreak | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/SceneBreak.tsx | * * * divider node |
| InlineComment | src/components/ManuscriptEditor/InlineComment.tsx | Highlighted text + tooltip annotation |

### Modified files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx | Replace textarea with ManuscriptEditor; swap Notes sidebar for SceneNavigator |
| src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css | Adjust sidebar width; add navigator open/close classes |
| src/hooks/useAutosave.ts | Add optional sceneId param; per-scene storage keys |
| src/lib/autosave.ts | Update key functions for sceneId; add legacy content migration helper |

New CSS modules (one per component): ManuscriptEditor, SceneSection, SceneHeader, SceneBody, SceneNavigator, RichTextToolbar, SceneBreak, InlineComment.

---

## Rich-Text Engine Recommendation

**Tiptap v2** (ProseMirror-based):
- Headless - no default styles, CSS modules apply cleanly
- React bindings (@tiptap/react) work with Next.js App Router
- Markdown shortcuts via @tiptap/extension-typography
- Inline comments via custom mark extension

Extensions: StarterKit (bold/italic/history only; disable heading, lists, blockquote, code, strike, horizontalRule) + @tiptap/extension-placeholder + @tiptap/extension-typography + custom SceneBreak node + custom InlineComment mark.

---

## Accessibility

title: Create lightweight timeline view for chronological consistency
description:
## Problem
Writers struggle to track chronology across scenes, causing continuity errors and rework late in the drafting process.

## Target user
Novelists managing multi-thread stories with time-sensitive events.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce reported continuity-conflict edits during revision.
- Increase number of stories using timeline metadata per scene.

## Scope
- Add optional timeline metadata to scenes (date/time or relative order).
- Provide a timeline view listing scenes in chronological order independent of manuscript order.
- Flag obvious chronology conflicts (e.g., duplicate impossible ordering within same storyline tag).
- Enable click-through from timeline entry to scene editor.

## Non-goals
- Full calendar app integration.
- Complex simulation of travel/logistics constraints.
- AI auto-fixing timeline conflicts.

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can set and edit timeline metadata for a scene.
- Timeline view renders scenes by chronological order and supports navigation to scene drafts.
- At least one deterministic chronology conflict rule is enforced and surfaced to the writer.
- Timeline metadata persists and survives refresh/reload.

## Priority rationale
Timeline support improves structural quality and reduces revision pain, making the app more credible for long-form fiction projects.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship draft milestone planner with chapter-level progress and finish-date forecast

## Target user

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active stories reaching “draft complete” status
- Improve week-over-week writing consistency for users who enable milestones
- Reduce abandonment rate after first 2 weeks of use

## Scope
- Add optional draft milestone setup: target draft length, target completion date, weekly writing cadence
- Compute chapter-level progress against draft plan and show remaining effort
- Surface a forecast indicator (on-track / at-risk) based on recent writing velocity

## Non-goals
- Public accountability/community leaderboards
- Punitive streak mechanics or gamification-heavy UX

## Acceptance criteria
- User can configure a milestone plan in under 2 minutes
- System shows current progress, next milestone, and forecast state for active story
- Forecast updates after writing sessions without manual refresh
- User can edit or disable milestone plan at any time

Onboarding gets users started, but completion mechanics keep them through the full manuscript lifecycle. Shipping this now strengthens retention and aligns with user need to finish stories, not just organize them.

Assign to: Market Research

title: Add chapter pacing board with length variance, tension tags, and gap alerts

Writers struggle to spot pacing problems until late revision because chapter/scene structure lacks a dedicated pacing view that highlights slow stretches, abrupt jumps, or missing tension progression.

## Target user
Novelists revising full drafts who need macro-level pacing signals before line edits.

- Reduce late-stage structural rewrites after beta feedback

- Add `Pacing` board at story level showing chapters and scenes with word-count distribution
- Support scene tension tags (`setup`, `build`, `peak`, `release`) and chapter-level pacing summary

Living Writer emphasizes structure, but pacing diagnostics are often handled outside drafting tools. A built-in pacing board creates a practical differentiation point: faster structural revision cycles inside the same writer workflow.

title: Build Beta Feedback Digest that clusters reader comments into actionable chapter insights
description:
## Problem
Raw beta-reader comments are valuable but noisy. Writers struggle to convert scattered feedback into prioritized revision decisions, so useful insight gets lost and revision cycles slow down.

## Target user
Writers using private chapter sharing who receive reader comments and need fast synthesis before revision passes.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of beta-feedback comments that result in completed revision actions
- Reduce time from feedback receipt to first revision action
- Increase repeat usage of chapter sharing + feedback workflow

## Scope
- Add a `Feedback Digest` view that groups incoming reader comments by chapter and theme (clarity, pacing, character, confusion, emotional impact)
- Provide confidence indicators based on repeated signals (e.g., 5 readers flagged pacing in Chapter 4)
- Allow writer to convert any digest cluster into a revision task in the Revision Queue or scene note
- Include reader-trust controls: exclude outlier comments and hide anonymous low-signal inputs
- Support export of digest summary for external editing review

## Non-goals
- Public social feed or story discovery marketplace
- Automatic acceptance/rejection of feedback without writer choice
- Replacing raw comment view entirely

## Acceptance criteria
- Digest groups feedback by chapter and at least five predefined theme buckets
- Writer can open a digest cluster and inspect underlying source comments
- Writer can convert a digest item into a revision task in one flow
- Writer can exclude a comment from digest weighting and see aggregate values recalculate
- Digest updates when new chapter comments arrive

## Priority rationale
Inkitt-like feedback loops are useful, but differentiation comes from turning feedback into decisive revision actions inside the writing workspace. This feature creates that bridge and strengthens our writer-first revision workflow.

## Handoff

title: Add Story Compass panel to keep premise, stakes, and promises visible while drafting

title: Ship scene version history with diff preview and safe restore
description:
## Problem
Writers need confidence that drafts are never lost and that risky revisions can be undone later. Current flows emphasize drafting speed, but there is no explicit manuscript history experience for restoring earlier versions.

## Target user
Writers iterating through multiple revision passes who want to experiment safely without copying text into external backups.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce incidents of users reporting lost or overwritten content
- Increase usage of revision workflows (edits per session, return edits after pause)
- Increase retention among users with multi-session projects

## Scope
- Add automatic scene snapshots at meaningful checkpoints (manual save, significant text delta, and pre-revision-pass actions).
- Add a Version History panel for each scene with timestamped entries and optional short labels.
- Provide side-by-side diff preview (current vs selected snapshot) before restore.
- Allow one-click restore of a snapshot into the active scene with confirmation.
- Keep a short audit trail of restore actions in history.

## Non-goals
- Full Git-like branching/merge model
- Cross-story compare views
- Collaborative multi-author conflict resolution in this ticket

## Acceptance criteria
- Scene history list shows timestamped snapshots in reverse chronological order.
- Writer can open any snapshot and preview text differences before restoring.
- Restore action requires explicit confirmation and creates a new latest snapshot (so restores are reversible).
- Snapshot creation and restore do not noticeably block editor typing performance.
- History remains available after refresh/reopen of the same story.

## Priority rationale
Trust is foundational for long-form writing products. A robust history flow reduces fear of revision and strengthens differentiation versus lightweight editors that do not protect iterative drafting deeply.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

[Design]:
## UX goal
Give writers a calm, trust-first “Version History” flow inside the scene editor so they can experiment aggressively, compare revisions quickly, and restore safely without fear of data loss.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: current scene writing surface remains dominant.
2. Secondary: version timeline and selected snapshot metadata in a right-side panel (desktop) / slide-over (mobile).
3. Tertiary: diff preview and restore controls, shown only when a snapshot is selected.
4. Audit context: recent restore events visible in timeline as distinct entries.

## Layout and components
- Keep the existing editor shell in [src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx] and [src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css] with a 2-column desktop editor layout.
- Add a new “History” section in the existing editor sidebar area (`.sidebar`) so Notes and History can be stacked sections (or tabbed if vertical space is constrained).
- History section structure:
  - Header row: “Version History” + lightweight status text (e.g., “Auto-saved just now”).
  - Reverse-chronological list of snapshot rows (timestamp, optional label, restore badge when applicable).
  - Snapshot row selection opens diff preview region below list.
- Diff preview:
  - Side-by-side panes: “Current draft” and “Selected version”.
  - Preserve readable prose width and relaxed line-height; avoid dense code-like diff visuals.
  - Inline emphasis for changed blocks only (minimal highlighting, not every character).
- Restore action:
  - Primary button in diff footer: “Restore this version”.
  - Confirmation dialog with consequence copy and explicit CTA.
  - Post-restore success banner in sidebar: “Version restored. A new snapshot was created.”

## Interaction states
- Idle: list shows latest snapshots; newest entry appears as active baseline.
- Loading: skeleton rows in history list and muted placeholders in diff panes.
- Empty: concise message (“No snapshots yet. Snapshots appear automatically as you write.”).
- Error: inline error block in panel with retry action; editor remains fully usable.
- Success: non-blocking confirmation after restore; timeline prepends a new restore snapshot and labels it.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard:
  - Snapshot rows are tabbable buttons; Enter/Space selects snapshot.
  - Restore confirmation is fully keyboard-operable with focus trap and Escape close.
- Focus:
  - Use current focus treatment pattern (2px outline) consistent with existing components.
  - After restore, move focus to success banner or restored snapshot row with clear announcement.
- Contrast:
  - Use approved grayscale tokens with at least AA contrast for body text and controls.
- Text clarity:
  - Writer-facing labels should be plain language (“Current draft”, “Saved 2:14 PM”, “Restored”).

## Visual style
- Match existing grayscale system in [src/styles/tokens.css]:
  - Background surfaces: `--color-white`, `--color-polar-100`
  - Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`
  - Primary text: `--color-black-700`
  - Secondary/meta text: `--color-elephant-500` / `--color-cement-400`
  - Interactive hover/focus surfaces: `--color-fog-200`
- Maintain current minimalist, low-noise style and serif writing canvas in editor.
- Avoid adding accent colors for this feature; trust should come from hierarchy and copy clarity, not chroma.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024px): persistent sidebar history panel alongside editor.
- Tablet (768-1023px): keep editor-first layout; history can be a collapsible panel below header or a narrow slide-over to avoid squeezing text area.
- Mobile (<768px): history opens from a one-tap control in editor header as a full-height sheet; diff uses stacked panes (Current first, Selected second).

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed and aligned with current app structure:
  - Route/layout: [src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx], [src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css], [src/app/layout.tsx]
  - Shell/navigation/container: [src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx], [src/components/Header/Header.tsx], [src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx]
  - Style primitives/tokens: [src/styles/tokens.css]
  - Existing interaction patterns: [src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx], [src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx], [src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx]
- Recommendation is compatible with current CSS-module + token architecture; no framework or design-system shift required.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - Existing editor page layout, sidebar container, header spacing, focus styles, token palette.
  - Existing button/input visual language from current CSS modules.
- New build:
  - Scene history panel component(s) (timeline list, snapshot item, diff preview, restore confirmation dialog, restore success banner).
  - Snapshot state model/hooks integrated with scene data layer.

## Feasibility notes
- Snapshot list virtualization may be needed if histories grow large; otherwise scrolling in sidebar should suffice for v1.
- Diff rendering should avoid full-document reflow while typing; compute/refresh on snapshot selection, not every keystroke.

title: Add distraction-free Focus Mode for deep drafting sessions
description:
## Problem
Writers lose momentum when the editor UI competes for attention, especially during long drafting sessions. The current product has strong planning surfaces but lacks an explicit distraction-free drafting mode similar to the best writer-first tools.

## Target user
Daily fiction and long-form writers who do focused drafting sprints and want a clean, low-noise writing environment.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase average words written per active writing session
- Increase percentage of sessions longer than 20 minutes
- Increase weekly active writers who complete at least 3 focused sessions

## Scope
- Add a toggleable Focus Mode in the editor that minimizes non-essential UI (left/right side panels, secondary controls, visual noise).
- Keep only core drafting controls visible: title, body editor, exit focus mode, and minimal save/status indicator.
- Add typewriter-scroll option (cursor line stays in upper-middle viewport) with persisted preference.
- Add optional session timer presets (15/30/45 minutes) with lightweight end-of-session summary (time and words written).
- Persist focus preferences per user/browser.

## Non-goals
- Gamification systems (badges, streak rewards, social leaderboards)
- AI writing assistance changes in this ticket
- Redesigning the full editor outside Focus Mode

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can enter and exit Focus Mode from the story editor with one action.
- In Focus Mode, non-essential panels are hidden and the editor remains fully usable with keyboard shortcuts.
- Typewriter-scroll can be toggled on/off and persists across refresh.
- Session timer can start from preset durations and shows elapsed/remaining time without blocking writing.
- Exiting a completed timer session shows a summary with words written in that session.

## Priority rationale
This is a core differentiator for writer trust and daily habit formation. Living Writer’s clarity is a key inspiration; shipping a superior focused drafting experience now can improve retention before expanding more advanced tooling.

## Handoff

Create a one-action Focus Mode that removes chrome and keeps writers in uninterrupted drafting flow, while preserving confidence signals (save state, timer state, easy exit).

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: story title + body editor (always visible in Focus Mode).
2. Secondary: minimal focus utility rail (exit focus, typewriter toggle, timer status/control).
3. Tertiary: save/status indicator (visible but low prominence).
4. Hidden in Focus Mode: global nav, app header, right notes sidebar, view mode switch, other non-drafting controls.

## Layout and components
- In normal editor view, retain current two-column editor + notes layout.
- Entering Focus Mode collapses to a single centered drafting canvas with generous horizontal margins and reduced visual boundaries.
- Keep title input at top of canvas, then body editor occupying remaining height.
- Add a compact top-right utility cluster inside the story editor canvas (not global app header):
  - Exit Focus button
  - Typewriter scroll toggle
  - Timer control (preset picker + running state)
  - Save/status text
- Timer completion summary should appear as a lightweight inline sheet anchored near the utility cluster (non-blocking; dismissible; keyboard focusable).

## Interaction states
- Idle: clean canvas, muted utility controls until hover/focus, active writing cursor in editor.
- Loading: preserve editor skeleton shape and keep title/editor placeholders in final positions to avoid layout shift.
- Empty: editor placeholder copy remains instructional and calm; timer controls still available.
- Error: show compact inline error near utility cluster (e.g., timer persistence failure) without covering editor text.
- Success: timer-complete summary shows session duration + words written, then collapses back to minimal status after dismissal/timeout.

- Keyboard: one-action enter/exit must be reachable via keyboard; utility controls tab in logical order before editor body.
- Focus: strong visible focus ring using existing dark grayscale token, including on compact icon/button controls.
- Contrast: keep text and controls at AA contrast against white/polar backgrounds; avoid low-contrast timer text.

- Tablet: keep single-column focus canvas; timer/typewriter controls remain visible, compact spacing.
- Mobile: Focus Mode still one action; keep only essential controls in a single-row compact bar above editor, no floating overlapping panels.

title: Add read-aloud revision mode with pacing, repetition, and dialogue clarity highlights
description:
## Problem
Many narrative issues (awkward cadence, repeated phrasing, unnatural dialogue beats) are hard to catch while silently reading. Writers need a revision pass tuned for voice and flow quality.

## Target user
Drafting-stage novelists and editors polishing prose quality before beta-reader sharing or submission.

## Outcome / KPI
- 25% increase in usage of revision tooling per active manuscript.
- 20% decrease in repeat phrasing flags in final manuscript versions.
- 15% faster time from first draft to “ready for feedback” milestone.

## Scope
- Add a read-aloud mode at chapter/scene level with play/pause and sentence-follow cursor.
- Highlight potential prose issues during playback: repeated phrases, long-run sentences, and dense dialogue blocks lacking beats/tags.
- Provide quick actions from each highlight: jump to sentence, dismiss, or mark for later.
- Add “Revision pass summary” with counts by issue type and completion progress.
- Preserve writer control: all suggestions are optional and reversible.

## Non-goals
- Fully autonomous rewriting of scenes.
- Language translation or multilingual grammar tooling.
- Accessibility-grade screen reader replacement.

## Acceptance criteria
- User can run read-aloud mode on any scene/chapter and see synchronized sentence focus.
- System surfaces at least three issue classes (repetition, long sentence cadence, dialogue clarity) with clickable locations.
- Dismissing an issue removes it from active list without editing text automatically.
- Revision summary updates after edits and supports rerun.
- Feature remains usable without enabling broader AI generation features.

## Priority rationale
Living Writer-style structure helps writers organize stories; this feature improves prose finish quality directly in-app, creating a stronger “draft to publish-ready” workflow and a differentiated quality pass beyond basic outlining.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

## UX goal
Create a focused revision pass that helps writers hear and fix prose flow issues (cadence, repetition, dialogue clarity) without breaking drafting momentum. Read-aloud should feel like a temporary assistive layer on top of the existing editor, not a separate complicated tool.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: manuscript text area with sentence-follow focus during playback.
2. Secondary: compact playback controls (play/pause, speed, sentence progress, rerun scan).
3. Tertiary: issue list grouped by type (Repetition, Long sentence cadence, Dialogue clarity) with per-item actions (Jump, Dismiss, Mark for later).
4. Summary layer: revision pass summary (total issues, by type, resolved/dismissed/remaining) visible in the right panel.

## Layout and components
- Keep current editor-first layout in story view: main editor + right sidebar.
- In editor mode header, place a one-click "Read Aloud" toggle near existing view controls; playback controls appear inline when active.
- Use lightweight sentence highlight in the editor while audio plays (single active sentence + subtle issue underlines for flagged spans).
- Convert current sidebar placeholder into a "Revision" panel with:
  - summary card at top
  - issue-type filter tabs/chips
  - scrollable issue list with jump/dismiss/mark actions
- Keep corkboard view unchanged for v1 except optional entry affordance to open revision mode from scene context.

## Interaction states
- Idle: revision panel collapsed to a clear CTA ("Run read-aloud pass").
- Loading: show skeleton rows in revision panel and disabled playback controls with "Analyzing scene…" status text.
- Empty: explicit copy when no issues are found ("Great flow — no cadence/repetition/dialogue flags detected") plus rerun action.
- Error: non-blocking inline alert in panel (analysis/playback failed) with retry; editor remains fully usable.
- Success: issue list and summary update immediately after dismiss/mark, with lightweight confirmation text and undo for recent dismiss.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: all playback and issue actions must be reachable via tab order and activatable by Enter/Space.
- Focus: when "Jump" is selected, move focus to editor and visibly focus the target sentence; preserve return path to issue list.
- Contrast: use existing grayscale tokens with at least readable contrast for text and focus rings; do not rely on color alone for issue type distinction (add labels/icons/text badges).
- Text clarity: concise, writer-friendly labels (avoid NLP jargon); sentence-length warnings should explain "why flagged" in plain language.

## Visual style
- Match current minimalist grayscale UI and border language from tokens in `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Surfaces: `--color-white` for primary editor cards, `--color-polar-100` for panel/background sections.
- Dividers/borders: `--color-fashion-300`; interactive emphasis/focus: `--color-carbon-600`; primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary copy: `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Keep prose-first typography in editor body (existing serif writing area), with sans-serif utility text in controls/panel for scannability.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024px): keep two-column editor + revision panel with sticky playback controls at top of editor region.
- Tablet (768-1023px): hide persistent sidebar by default; revision panel opens as a slide-over panel to protect writing width.
- Mobile (<768px): revision UI becomes a bottom sheet/full-height panel; sentence jump returns to editor and closes sheet unless user pins it open.

- Reviewed current shell and editor structure in:
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`

  - existing story editor route/layout and title input pattern
  - existing focus styles, spacing, and grayscale tokens
  - existing toolbar interaction conventions from view switch and corkboard toolbars
- New build:
  - read-aloud control cluster
  - sentence-sync highlight treatment in editor content
  - revision issue list + summary module in sidebar
  - issue-type badges/filter controls specific to revision pass

- Current editor is a plain `textarea`; precise sentence-level highlight and in-text flagged spans are not feasible inside native textarea rendering.

title: Create submission package builder for query letter, synopses, and beta-reader packet
description:
## Problem
Writers can draft manuscripts but still face major friction when preparing submission deliverables. They must manually assemble query letters, synopses, and beta-reader materials in separate tools, causing inconsistency and duplicated effort.

## Target user
Writers approaching submission-readiness who need a repeatable handoff workflow from manuscript to external deliverables.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of completed manuscripts that generate at least one submission package
- Reduce time from draft-complete to first external submission/share event
- Improve perceived end-to-end workflow satisfaction

## Scope
- Add `Submission Package` workspace at story level
- Generate editable templates for: query letter, one-page synopsis, long synopsis, and beta-reader briefing sheet
- Auto-link core manuscript metadata (title, genre, logline, comps, protagonist/conflict placeholders) into templates
- Include versioning for package drafts with timestamps and status labels (draft, ready, submitted)
- Export package as DOCX bundle and plain-text copy blocks

## Non-goals
- Agent/discovery marketplace integrations
- Automated email sending or CRM workflow
- Legal contract generation

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create a submission package from an existing story in under 5 minutes
- All package documents are editable and save independently
- Metadata changes can be refreshed into templates with explicit user confirmation
- Writer can export package artifacts without losing template structure
- Package status and version history persist per story

## Priority rationale
Market Research identified publishing handoff as a concrete gap where competitors are weaker. This feature expands the app from drafting tool to full manuscript workflow, improving adoption and retention near the finish line.

Enable a writer to create, edit, and export a complete submission package in under 5 minutes from the story workspace, with a flow that feels as direct as drafting.

3. Document workspace: Query Letter, One-Page Synopsis, Long Synopsis, Beta-Reader Briefing as first-class editable tabs/cards.
4. Version history: chronological list of saved versions with status badges and restore/view affordance.
5. Supporting metadata panel: title, genre, logline, comps, protagonist/conflict placeholders with clear source-of-truth messaging.

## Layout and components
- Add a story-level mode switch option for `Submission Package` alongside existing `Editor`/`Corkboard` patterns (same one-click interaction model).
- Use a two-column desktop layout aligned to current story page composition:
  - Main column: selected document editor with template scaffold and inline section headings.

- Document navigation should use a compact segmented/tab row (4 document types) anchored above the editor body.

- Idle:

- Loading:

- Empty:
  - No package yet: single callout card with 3-step explanation (generate, edit, export) and one primary CTA.
  - Empty version history: helper copy “Versions appear after first save.”
- Error:
  - Inline banner in main column (non-modal) with plain-language failure and retry action.
  - Export failures should preserve current edits and show retry without page reset.
- Success:
  - Non-blocking confirmation toast/inline notice after save, metadata refresh, and export.
  - Version creation confirms timestamp + status badge added to history.

- Keyboard:

- Focus:

- Contrast:
  - Keep text/background combinations at AA minimum using existing grayscale tokens.
  - Status badges need text contrast distinct from surrounding card backgrounds.
- Text clarity:

- Tablet (768-1023px): collapse right rail below editor as stacked cards; keep document tabs fixed at top of content.
- Mobile (<768px): single-column flow; tabs become horizontally scrollable pills; actions prioritize `Save` and `Export` with secondary options under overflow.

title: Create shareable read-only chapter preview links for private feedback
description:
## Problem
Writers need lightweight external feedback before publishing, but the app currently lacks a safe, low-friction way to share draft chapters without granting full edit access.

## Target user
Solo writers seeking feedback from trusted readers/critique partners during drafting and revision.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of stories that receive at least one external feedback cycle
- Reduce time from draft completion to first feedback received
- Improve retention of writers in revision stages

## Scope
- Add per-chapter `Share preview` action that creates a read-only link
- Allow writer to set optional expiration date and regenerate/revoke links
- Preview page shows chapter content only (no editor controls, no internal workspace)
- Capture lightweight reader reactions (thumbs up/down + short comment)
- Surface feedback entries back in writer’s chapter sidebar

## Non-goals
- Full public publishing/discovery feed
- Real-time collaborative editing permissions
- Advanced moderation/community management systems

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can generate, copy, and revoke a preview link per chapter
- Link opens a read-only page accessible without app account
- Expired/revoked links no longer expose chapter content
- Reader feedback submitted through preview appears in writer view within the chapter context
- Sharing controls are clearly scoped to chapter-level content only

## Priority rationale
This introduces an Inkitt-inspired feedback loop in a controlled, private form that fits MVP progression and helps writers iterate faster without expanding into full community infrastructure.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship manuscript export to DOCX and PDF with chapter-aware formatting
description:
## Problem
Writers need to share or submit manuscripts outside the app, but there is no dependable export path that preserves chapter structure and readable formatting. This creates lock-in anxiety and blocks practical adoption.

## Target user
Drafting and revising writers who need to send manuscripts to beta readers, editors, or submission channels in standard document formats.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of active stories that reach an export event
- Reduce churn risk signals related to data portability concerns
- Improve user satisfaction for end-to-end drafting workflow

## Scope
- Add `Export Manuscript` action at story level
- Support exports to `.docx` and `.pdf`
- Export includes title page (optional), chapter headings, scene breaks, and body text in reading order
- Provide basic formatting presets: manuscript standard and compact reading copy
- Include export confirmation with file type and timestamp

## Non-goals
- Query-package generation (synopsis, pitch letters, submission bundles)
- Advanced style customization controls
- Collaborative annotation inside exported documents

## Acceptance criteria
- User can export any story to DOCX and PDF without app crash or data loss
- Chapter/scene ordering in export matches in-app structure
- Formatting preset choice is applied correctly in output documents
- Exported files are downloadable locally with deterministic filenames
- Failed exports surface actionable error messaging and allow retry

## Priority rationale
Data portability is table-stakes for serious writers and directly supports trust in a writer-first product. A scoped, reliable export feature closes a core workflow gap without overreaching into publishing-suite complexity.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Reframe as story-aware focus sessions tied to scene objectives
description:
## Problem
Generic writing timers are now category-standard and do not reliably improve story completion quality on their own. Writers need focused sessions that connect directly to scene-level narrative outcomes, not just elapsed time and word counts.

## Target user
Fiction writers who draft in scenes and want structured sessions that help them finish specific narrative objectives (new beat, revision pass, dialogue polish) with less context-switching.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of sessions with completed scene objective
- Increase multi-session continuity rate (users resume the suggested next scene within 24h)
- Improve week-2 retention among users who run story-aware focus sessions

## Scope
- Add `Story-aware Focus Session` mode in the editor with optional timer presets (15/25/45 minutes)
- Require selecting a target scene and objective type before starting (e.g., draft beat, revise pacing, tighten dialogue)
- Show live progress with both words-added and objective status (in progress / complete)
- End session with recap: words added, objective completion, and suggested next-scene handoff note
- Persist recent session history per story, including objective metadata

## Non-goals
- Social leaderboards, streak competitions, or multiplayer battles
- Advanced coaching/critique generated by AI
- Cross-story analytics dashboards in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- User can start/pause/end a focus session tied to a selected scene without data loss
- Objective selection is required at session start and stored in session history
- Session recap includes objective completion status and a next-step handoff note
- Save-state visibility remains present during active session mode
- Session settings/history persist across reload for the same user and story

## Priority rationale
Competitors already offer timer-only sprint tools; reframing around scene-objective completion creates a clearer story-first advantage and aligns with our goal to ship a writer workflow optimized for drafting outcomes.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
